description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 89 D2 Apostoli, Mon.

name: Aqaba
description: Aqaba - now Aaqbe - is a place located in the Beqaa Governorate in Lebanon. It is the site of a Roman temple that has been classified as belonging to the "Mount Hermon" temple group.

name: Aqaba Church
description: A pre-Constantinian Christian church located at ancient Aila in Jordan.

name: Aquae Segetae
description: An ancient settlement at modern Sceaux-du-Gâtinais in France (BAtlas 11 C4 Sceaux-du-Gâtinais)

name: Ara della Regina
description: A fourth to third century B.C. sanctuary at Tarquinia.

name: Arachnaion (mountain)
description: A mountain of the Argolid with a higher western peak (Profitis Ilias, 1199 masl) and a lower eastern peak (Mavrovouni or Arna, 1139 masl). Material from the Mycenaean, Archaic, and Classical periods has been found on the western peak and identified with altars of Zeus and Hera described by Pausanias. Aeschylus mentions its use as a signal station.

name: Archegetes, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 D4 Archegetes, T.

name: Archilocheion
description: A sanctuary dedicated to the Greek lyric poet Archilochus, known as the Archilocheion, was established on Paros during the third century BCE. It is attested textually in both literature and inscriptions; however, the precise location of the sanctuary has not been identified. During the 19th and 20th centuries, some scholars argued for it to be placed at the site of the “Three Churches” (Tris Ekklisies) because inscriptions mentioning Archilochos were found reused in remains of the 6th century Christian basilica over which the later churches had been built. Other scholars (e.g., Kontoleon in PECS) have vigorously rejected this view. Foss (BAtlDir) cites Berranger in tentatively placing the Archilocheion further east at the modern village of Elitas.

name: Arcia di Niscemi
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 47 E4 Arcia di Niscemi

name: Area archeologica di Colle della Noce
description: The ancient temple in the sacred area of Colle della Noce dates to the fifth and fourth century BCE.

name: Ares, Aphrodite, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 60 E2 Ares, Aphrodite, T.

name: Aron
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 H4 Aron

name: Arsameia pros Nymphaio
description: An ancient fortified city on high ground on the south bank of the river Nymphaios (modern Kâhta Çayı) south of the modern village of Kocahisar (also known as Eski Kahta) in Turkey.

name: Arteda Civitate
description: An early medieval settlement in Spain (9th to 13th centuries CE) listed in contemporary Latin documents. This settlement is now identified with a second phase of occupation of an archaeological site known as El Forau de la Tuta near modern Arieda in Zaragosa province where a previously unknown Roman settlement has also been discovered. In addition to archaeological finds in situ, spolia from both the Roman and medieval phases have been identified in the more recent Ermita de San Pedro.

name: Artemis Diktynnaia, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 D4 Artemis Diktynnaia, T.

name: Artemis Elaphebolia, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 D3 Artemis Elaphebolia, T.

name: Artemis Hegemone, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 B3 Artemis Hegemone, T.

name: Artemis Laphria Sanctuary (Kalydon)
description: This sanctuary is believed to have been active since at least the Geometric period, possibly on the site of a Mycenaean settlement. Ceramic evidence indicates continuous activity at least through the Roman period.

name: Artemis Parthenos, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 D3 Artemis Parthenos, T.

name: Artemis Tauropolos temple
description: Near Artemida/Loutsa in modern Attiki, the archaeological remains of a temple in the Doric order were first identified in 1956. Excavators identified the sanctuary as belonging to Artemis Tauropolos. The compilers of the Barrington Atlas, in keeping with the discussion offered in PECS by C.W.J. Eliot, associate the temple site with the deme of Halai Araphenides.

name: Artemis Tauropolos, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 C2 Artemis Tauropolos, T.

name: Artemis Thermia, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 C3 Artemis Thermia, T.

name: Artemision
description: The possible site of the temple of Artemis Proseoa beneath the Ag. Georgios chapel, Artemision (Kourbatsi) Euboea.

name: Arthur's O'On
description: A circular Roman temple that stood on the river Carron until it was destroyed in 1743.

name: Arx of Cosa
description: The citadel of the ancient city and location of its Capitolium.

name: Asabaa
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 35 E2 Asabaa

name: Asbamaion
description: A sanctuary sacred to Zeus (Jupiter) Asbamaeus that was located in Cappadocia. According to Ammianus Marcellinus, a sacred spring could be seen at the sanctuary. Apollonius of Tyana was said to have been born here.

name: Asea Vigla
description: A Doric temple dated to the sixth century BCE.

name: Ash Altar of Zeus Lykaios
description: The ash altar of Zeus Lykaios on the southern peak of Mt. Lykaion.

name: Asklepeion of Ancient Trichoneion
description: An excavated sanctuary sacred to Asklepios located at Gavalou in Aitoloakarnania West Greece.

name: Asklepieion (Kos)
description: A major, ancient sanctuary of Asklepios located on the Aegean island of Kos.

name: Asklepieion at Zea
description: A sanctuary of Asklepios above Zea harbour in Piraeus, founded in the late 5th century BC and active through the Imperial period. Sections of the sanctuary were explored archaeologically in the 1880s and 2000s.

name: Asklepieion of Messene
description: The Asklepieion in Ancient Messene is a sanctuary of Asklepios, the Greek god of healing, constructed in 214 BC. The complex includes civic as well as sacred buildings

name: Asklepieion of Pheneos
description: The Asklepieion of Pheneos dates to the later second century BCE. It was identified via excavation in the late 1950s.

name: Asklepieion of Trikka
description: The Asklepieion of Trikka was said to be the oldest such sanctuary in Greece.

name: Asklepieion, Thesmophorion?, Apollo, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 A3 Asklepieion, Thesmophorion?, Apollo, T.

name: Asklepios, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 B4 Asklepios, T.

name: Aspripetra
description: A cavernous grotto on the island of Kos that seems to have served as a place of worship in ancient times. Pan and the Nymphs were venerated there in Archaic Greek times. The grotto's ancient name is unknown.

name: Atessa/Porcari Sanctuary
description: A small Italic sanctuary, possibly of Vejovis, in a modern area known then as Passo Porcari (between Monte Marcone and Saletti) near Atessa in Italy's Chieti province. It was excavated in the 1970s and is memorialized by the modern street name "Via Veiove". Rescue excavations of purportedly medieval finds were carried out in the immediate vicinity between 2017 and 2020 ahead of a road modification.

name: Athena Itonia?, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 D4 Athena Itonia?, T.

name: Athena Kranaia, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 D3 Athena Kranaia, T.

name: Athena Poliouchos, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 A3 Athena Poliouchos, T.

name: Athena, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 B3 Athena, T.

name: Athena, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 60 F2 Athena, T.

name: Athena, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 C3 Athena, T.

name: Athena, Zeus?, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 A5 Athena, Zeus?, T.

name: Athribis/Triph(i)eion
description: Athribis/Triph(i)eion in Upper Egypt, located outside modern Sohag, was the site of a temple built for the goddess Repyt (Triphis) in the Ptolemaic period.

name: Athroula/Laththa
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 4 B2 Athroula/Laththa

name: Augusteum at Carthago Nova/Col. Urbs Iulia
description: A temple dedicated to the imperial cult during the reign of Augustus.

name: Augusteum, Antioch in Pisidia
description: The Augusteum, or Sebasteion, at Antioch in Pisidia was a monumental architectural complex dedicated to the Roman Imperial cult. Its archaeological remains were first excavated in 1913. Approximately 60 fragments of the Res Gestae Divi Augusti were discovered at the site.

name: Ayios Filon
description: A ruined fifth-century CE basilica on the Karpass peninsula of Cyprus.

name: Ayios Photios
description: Caches of sculpture discovered in the 19th century by Luigi di Cesnola provide evidence for a cult of a male deity in the area of the modern Ayios Photios church near Athienou on Cyprus.

name: Ayn-Manawir Temple
description: Temple dedicated to Osiris located 3km west of the modern village of Dush.

name: Baal-Hamman, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 C2 Baal-Hamman, T.

name: Bachkovo Monastery
description: Monastery in southern Bulgaria founded in 1083 CE.

name: Bad Bertrich
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 11 H2 Bad Bertrich

name: Bağin
description: Village where fragments of a Urartian stele have been found (one in a church, one in a fortress).

name: Bailleul-sur-Thérain
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 11 C3 Bailleul-sur-Thérain

name: Baitokaike
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 68 B4 Baitokaike

name: Baldersdorf
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 19 F3 Baldersdorf

name: Balmarcodes, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 69 C2 Balmarcodes, T.

name: Balsignano
description: A fortified Medieval site located in Puglia.

name: Banteay Srei
description: Buddhist temple at Angkor, located approximately 20 km northeast of the main temple complex. Dedicated to Shiva, the temple was constructed during the 10th century CE during the reigns of Rajendravarman II and Jayavarman V.

name: Baptistery of Butrint
description: The Baptistery of Butrint is a round structure, with a diameter of 14.5 meters. It was built in the sixth century A.D. and was excavated in May 1928 by Luigi Maria Ugolini.

name: Baqirha
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 67 D4 Baqirha

name: Barbar Temples
description: Archaeological site in northern Bahrain associated with the Dilmun culture. Three successive temples were built on the site during the 3rd-2nd millennia BCE.

name: Bard-i Nishande
description: Bard-i Nishande is the site of a ruined ancient landscape consisting of multiple complexes. According to K. Schippmann, these complexes include a ruler's residence or "castle", a terraced platform used for ritual activity, and a lower town.

name: Barlaamus
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 68 A2 Barlaamus

name: Baron-sur-Odon
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 7 F2 Baron-sur-Odon

name: Barzan (Gallo-Roman Site)
description: Site of a Gallo-Roman sanctuary and subsequent city of the Roman imperial period, the "Site gallo-romain de Barzan" is spread across the modern French communes of Barzan, Talmont-sur-Gironde and Arces-sur-Gironde in the department of Charente-Maritime. BAtlas equated it with ancient Tannum/Tamnum, but alternatives continue to be offered.

name: Basilica and convent of S. Ippolito
description: An important paleochristian complex located in the area of Portus.

name: Basílica Casa Herrera
description: The Casa Herrera Basilica is part of the UNESCO World Heritage Site "Archaeological Ensemble of Mérida". The paleochristian basilica was built in either the late fifth or early sixth century CE.

name: Basílica de Son Peretó
description: A Paleochristian site dating to the fifth century A.D.

name: Basílica des Fornàs de Torelló
description: Basílica des Fornàs de Torelló, a sixth-century CE paleochristian basilica with mosaic pavements located in Mahon, Minorca.

name: Basilica di Santa Sinforosa
description: A minor Paleochristian basilica located in Guidonia Montecelio that was dedicated to the patron saint of Tivoli.

name: Basilica di Santo Stefano in via Latina
description: The now-ruined basilica of Santo Stefano in Via Latina was a fifth-century CE early Christian basilica. It is now located within the confines of the archaeological park of the Via Latina tombs.

name: Basilica Manastirine
description: An important extra-mural Christian site at Solina. The site includes the basilica and the cemetery of Manastirine, associated with a Christian martyr.

name: Basilica of Iomnium
description: The basilica of Iomnium (Tigzirt) likely dates to the fifth and sixth centuries CE.

name: Basilica of Lechaion
description: The basilica of Lechaion was dedicated to Leonides, the bishop of Athens. It is one of the most important early Byzantine monuments in the Peloponnese.

name: Basilica of Saint-Pierre-aux-Nonnains
description: A Christian basilica that incorporates the remains of a fourth-century AD Roman gymnasium.

name: Basilica of Sant'Apollinare in Classe
description: A basilica begun in the sixth century AD by Bishop Ursicinus with financial support from the Greek banker Iulianus Argentarius.

name: Basilica of St. Francis of Assisi
description: A papal basilica begun ca. AD 1228.

name: Basilica of the Sacred Heart (Paray-le-Monial)
description: 12th-century church in Paray-le-Monial, France. This site was a popular pilgrimage site and is an important example of Cluniac architecture.

name: Bau/Pboou
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 G4 Bau/Pboou

name: Bavian/Khinnis
description: Just a few kilometers upstream of the city, the 7th-century B.C. Assyrian king Sennacherib had an architectural complex built and several stele-like panels (with accompanying inscriptions) carved into a nearby cliff face. The site may have served as a summer residence for the Assyrian king and may have been used as a headquarters to supervise the construction of a vast network of canals and aqueducts that was created specifically for providing water to Nineveh and the Assyrian heartland. The "Khinnis Reliefs" were included on the 2014 World Monuments Watch to increase awareness of cultural heritage in the Khinnis/Bavian area.

name: Bayon at Angkor Thom
description: A temple in the Khmer capital of Angkor Thom, constructed in the late 12th or early 13th century CE.

name: Beaulieu
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 17 B3 Beaulieu

name: Beit el-Wali
description: Beit el-Wali, meaning "House of the Holy Man", was a site in Nubia where Ramesses II built a temple sacred to Amun-Re, Re-Horakhti, Khnum and Anuket. The temple was relocated in the 1960s.

name: Belbèze
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 G2 Belbèze

name: Bell-Loc d’Aro
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 H4 Bell-Loc d’Aro

name: Belmonte
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 45 E3 Belmonte

name: Belvedere Temple, Orvieto
description: A fifth century B.C. temple that was likely sacred to Tinia.

name: Beni Guedal
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 35 C2 Beni Guedal

name: Bertelles
description: A "fortified basilica" in Cyrenaica, probably dating to the 6th century CE and associated with nearby farmsteads and tombs, the latter dating to the Roman period, but reused in late antiquity.

name: Beşdut
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 86 E3 Beşdut

name: Bethabara
description: Bethabara, in modern Jordan, is an ancient site connected with John the Baptist. Bethabara is also attested on the sixth century A.D. "Madaba Map".

name: Biclaro?
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 F4 Biclaro?

name: Bidoni
description: A monumental sanctuary (26 x 20 m) dedicated to Iuppiter.

name: Binbirkilise
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 66 C2 Binbirkilise

name: Bir Abu Swera
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 76 D5 Bir Abu Swera

name: Bir Nesma
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 35 F3 Bir Nesma

name: Bishop Genesius Church
description: The Bishop Genesius Church at Jerash (Jordan) may be the earliest Christian church on the site. The church was built in the early seventh century, reusing a preexisting Roman temple.

name: Bitalemi
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 47 E4 Bitalemi

name: Bıçkıcı Kalesi
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 66 A4 Bıçkıcı Kalesi

name: Blatades Monastery
description: A fourteenth century monastery.

name: Boades
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 G4 Boades

name: Bostra/Col. Nova Traiana Alexandriana
description: The ancient city of Bostra (modern Busra in Syria), where extensive ancient Nabataean, Roman, Byzantine and Umayyad structures still visible. Inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1980.

name: Bou Kisnaden
description: This site, discussed in scholarly literature in the 20th century, seems from satellite imagery to have been impinged or destroyed in the construction of the Rocade Tipaza/Autoroute Tipaza - Cherchel in the early years of the 21st century. It seems to have been a villa with a central courtyard and primary entrance facing east, with a row of rooms with arched openings overlooking the wadi to the north. Vats and presses, possibly for processing grapes for wine, were discovered. Anselmino hypothesized that another, unexcavated structure immediately to the east was a church.

name: Bouleuterion of Onchestos
description: The Bouleuterion of Onchestos has been identified adjacent to the Temple of Poseidon.

name: Bracquemont
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 11 B3 Bracquemont

name: Brampton Old Church
description: The Brampton Old Church stands on the site of a Roman fort located 13 km northeast of Carlisle and 0.8 km south of the Stanegate.

name: Braq
description: Khirbet Braq is the modern name of a ruined Nabatean sanctuary at Ayn Braq in Jordan, a spring that, in antiquity, was channeled through an aqueduct to supply ancient Petra. The heavily looted site is located just south of modern Wadi Musa, on the west side of the modern road to Taybeh.

name: Brauron/sanctuary of Artemis Brauronia
description: Brauron was a sacred site in Attica dedicated to Artemis Brauronia. The sanctuary was active from the Middle Bronze Age period, although a gap in activity stretches from the Final Bronze Age to the eighth century B.C.

name: Brean Down
description: Brean Down is the site of a Romano-British temple that was built ca. A.D. 340 and destroyed about 50 years later. The site was excavated between 1957-8.

name: Breviglieri
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 35 F2 Breviglieri

name: Brides-les-Bains
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 17 H3 Brides-les-Bains

name: Brigstock
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 8 G2 Brigstock

name: Brihadishwara Temple
description: 11th century Hndu temple Tanjuvar dedicated to Shiva with smaller shrines around it.

name: Brikinniai?
description: A fortified ancient settlement near modern Lentini. It has been associated with the area of Monte San Basilio.

name: Britaniensis/Britonensis Ecclesia
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 24 D1 Britaniensis/Britonensis Ecclesia

name: Brittablo
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 24 H4 Brittablo

name: Bully
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 17 D2 Bully

name: Burgstall am Schlern cult site
description: A "Brandopferplatz" (Alpine burnt-offerings cult place) located at the so-called Burgstall, the topographically distinctive northern peak of the Schlern, a mountain in the South Tirolian Dolomites in modern Italy.

name: Burj al-Qa'im
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 91 E3 Burj al-Qa'im

name: Buscemi
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 47 F4 Buscemi

name: Büyük Çelebi
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 87 A4 Büyük Çelebi

name: Bziza
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 68 A5 Bziza

name: Caere
description: Caere (also known as Cisra or Cerveteri) was a major Etruscan polis located ca. 50 km north-northwest of Rome in southern Etruria.

name: Calalzo
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 19 E3 Calalzo

name: Camarina
description: Camarina was founded by Syracuse in 599 B.C., but subsequently destroyed in 552 B.C. The city was re-founded by Gela in 461 B.C. Carthage razed the site in 405 B.C., but Timoleon restored it in 339 B.C. From the late third century B.C. the town fell under Roman administration.

name: Camp celtique de la Bure
description: A Gallo-Roman settlement of a commercial nature located on the Meurthe river. Archaeological evidence for earthworks, as well as ritual activity, has been discovered. The Gallo-Roman ramparts, in a later phase, make use of marble sculpture as spolia.

name: Camp-Ferrus
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 G1 Camp-Ferrus

name: Campochiaro
description: An Italic sanctuary sacred to Hercules located in località Civitella in the comune of Campochiaro. The site is to be connected with the Pentrian Samnites.

name: Campoverde
description: Campoverde (Lazio) lies some 60 km south of Rome. In 1977-1978 a quantity of votive material was recovered from a body of water known as the "Laghetto del Monsignore". This spring-fed body of water was interpreted as an open-air votive depository.

name: Can Modolell
description: An ancient rural sanctuary of Hispania (Cabrera de Mar, Barcelona).

name: Cancho Roano
description: A pre-Roman sanctuary of the Iberian peninsula.

name: Cantobennum, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 I3 Cantobennum, Mon.

name: Capaccio
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 45 B4 Capaccio

name: Capitolium
description: The so-called Capitolium of Aquinum is found in the northwest part of the site. It may predate Roman colonization, and it is perhaps sacred to Iuno Regina.

name: Capitolium at Mactaris
description: The Capitolium temple at Mactaris likely dates to the reign of Marcus Aurelius.

name: Capitolium of Sala
description: Tetrastyle Corinthian temple located in the Roman forum at Sala (modern Chellah) dating from the early Hadrianic period (c.120 CE).

name: Capitolium of Sarsina
description: The remains of a first-century BCE temple podium discovered at Sarsina have been identified as the Roman settlement's Capitolium.

name: Capitolium of Verona
description: Excavations have determined the location of the capitolium of Roman Verona, part of an extensive, terraced complex that underpins medieval and later structures of the city. The capitolium itself stretches from the northern end of the modern Piazza Erbe to the via Emilei, lying beneath the Palazzo Maffei, church of San Benedetto, Palazzo Malaspina and surrounding structures.

name: Capo Cimiti
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 46 F4 Capo Cimiti

name: Capodifiume
description: Remains of a fourth-century B.C. sanctuary situated at a spring known as Caput aquae, from which the modern toponym is most likely derived.

name: Cappella di Sant'Antonino (Finale Ligure)
description: A twelfth-century chapel located near the Castrum Perticae. The site is located in the municipal territory of modern Finale Ligure in Italy's Ligurian Alps.

name: Carovilli
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 44 F2 Carovilli

name: Casalbore
description: At modern Casalbore, Italy, archaeological evidence includes a significant temple structure and associated material from the Samnite and Roman periods.

name: Casalicchio
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 47 D4 Casalicchio

name: Castelfusano Paleochristian basilica
description: Situated along the via Severiana are the remains of an anonymous Paleochristian basilica that was in use between the second and the seventh centuries AD.

name: Castellar
description: A Gallic oppidum and sanctuary to the god Dexiva in the Vaucluse départment.

name: Castellnou de Basella
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 G4 Castellnou de Basella

name: Castellu di Cucuruzzu
description: Castellu di Cucuruzzu is a Torrean archaeological site in Corsica.

name: Castelnau-Montratier
description: A Gallo-Roman site located in the Barguelonne valley that covers some 4,000 square meters. Excavation (1962-1964) suggested the site was likely a rural sanctuary that included a theater and baths.

name: Castiglione del Lago
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 42 C2 Castiglione del Lago

name: Castle Acre Priory
description: A priory founded in 1089 by William de Warenne and consecrated sometime between 1146 and 1148. Home of the first Cluniac order in England.

name: Cathedral of Santa Maria Assunta
description: The Cathedral of Santa Maria Assunta on the island of Torcello is a Roman Catholic basilica initially founded in 639. The second basilica was begun in 1008.

name: Cathedral of the Holy Cross at Aghtamar
description: Armenian church built on the island of Aghtamar by King Gagik I Artsruni between 915-921 CE.

name: Çatıören Church
description: Byzantine church remains at Çatıören in Rough Cilicia

name: Cattedrale di Santa Maria di Amiternum
description: The ruins of the cathedral of the fifth century are located in an area of the archaeological site of Amiternum that was occupied in the second and third centuries CE.

name: Cave 1 at Udayagiri
description: The southernmost cave at Udayagiri, dedicated to an unidentified god.

name: Cave of Pan and the Nymphs
description: A small cave, located northeast of Vari in Attica, Greece, that was used as a shrine dedicated to Apollo, Pan, and the Nymphs from the sixth to the second centuries BC.

name: Cengerli
description: A likely Roman fort and possible Urartian fire temple at the modern village of Cengerli in the Refahiye district of Erzincan province, Turkey.

name: Cerreto Sannita
description: A Samnite temple dedicated to Flora.

name: Cerro de los Santos
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 27 D3 Cerro de los Santos

name: Chafagi Aamer
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 35 F3 Chafagi Aamer

name: Chahar Deh
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 94 A3 Chahar Deh

name: Chale Ghar 1
description: Bronze Age copper mine reused as a sanctuary or ritual site from the 8th century BCE-8th century CE, located near the modern village of Veshnaveh, Iran.

name: Chalencon
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 17 D4 Chalencon

name: Chalia
description: Theopompus mentions Chalia as a town of Boeotia. It is also attested epigraphically and may have been a member of the Boeotian league. Fossey locates the city in the vicinity of the church of Agios Minas in modern Drosia. Archaeological finds in the area indicate human activity from as early as the Bronze Age.

name: Champlieu
description: A sanctuary located at the boundary of the civitas of the Suessiones and that of the Silvanectes.

name: Chanac
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 17 A5 Chanac

name: Chanceaux-sur-Choisille
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 F1 Chanceaux-sur-Choisille

name: Chapel of Hakor
description: Chapel for the Sacred Barque of Amun-Ra in Karnak, established by the Egyptian pharaoh Hakor of the Twenty-ninth dynasty who reigned from 392/1 to 379/8 BCE.

name: Chapelle Sainte-Agathe
description: The chapelle Sainte-Agathe was originally a Roman-period thermal complex that was converted to a Christian church by the seventh century.

name: Chariton
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 70 G2 Chariton

name: Château de Chatillon
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 G1 Château de Chatillon

name: Château-Bas

name: Châteaubleau
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 11 D4 Châteaubleau

name: Châtelard de Lardiers
description: A Celto-Ligurian oppidum and sanctuary.

name: Chettaba
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 31 E4 Chettaba

name: Chiesa di San Paolo di Peltuinum
description: The church of San Paolo at Peltuinum likely dates to the seventh or eighth century CE.

name: Chiesa di Santa Pudenziana, Narni
description: The church of Santa Pudenziana near Narni, Italy, dates from the ninth to eleventh centuries CE and reuses spoliated architectural elements from a Roman villa.

name: Chiesa Ipogea di San Nicola
description: A poorly understood paleochristian site located on the grounds of the Inviolata park in the comune of Guidonia-Montecelio in the greater metropolitan area of Rome. The cave site may originally have functioned as a mithraeum.

name: Chordiraza?/Baisampse?
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 67 G3 Chordiraza?/Baisampse?

name: Choziba
description: A desert monastery, founded in the fifth century CE, which was located where the 19th-century Greek Orthodox monastery of Deir Mar Jirys now stands on the north cliffs above the Wadi Qelt/Kelt in the West Bank, nearly 5km west from the center of modern Jericho.

name: Chrysas, T.
description: According to Cicero, the shrine of Chrysas (the god shares a name with the nearby river) was located along the road from Assorus to Henna. A cult image in marble, described as being "beautiful", was to be found there and was ultimately spared from the depredations of Caius Verres.

name: Church and early Christian necropolis at Vaste
description: An early Christian complex that included a cult building with several construction phases and a vast cemetery.

name: Church at Shams el-Din
description: The church, well visible in Google Earth, is in the SW sector of the archaeological area

name: Church at Trimithis
description: A structure at the city of Trimithis (Amheida) in the Dakhleh Oasis, identified as a church and dated to the 4th century CE.

name: Church in Sidi Khiribech
description: Ruins of a church likely built around the fifth century AD, on the summit of the low hill of Sidi Khiribech in Berenike. The character of the building and its position has suggested to some archaeologists a defensive as well as a religious purpose.

name: Church of Mren
description: 7th-century Armenian church in the Kars region of Turkey. This church is now ruined, but still is the second-largest domed basilica from the 7th-century Caucasus still extant.

name: Church of Saint Eusebius (Ronciglione)
description: A paleochristian sanctuary located along the Via Francigena.

name: Church of Saint Gregory of Tigran
description: Armenian church in Ani, in modern Eastern Turkey, completed in 1215.

name: Church of Saint Sophia
description: The Church of Saint Sophia, also referred to as the Old Bishopric, is an Eastern Orthodox church located in Nesebar, eastern Bulgaria. The church originally dates to the sixth century.

name: Church of San Primitivo
description: The so-called Church of San Primitivo in Monte Compatri. The church's bell tower was converted to a defensive tower around 1200.

name: Church of St. Etchmiadzin at Soradir
description: Sixth-century Armenian church in the town of Soradir (modern Yanal, Turkey).

name: Church of St. George
description: One of a group of eleven rock-hewn monolithic churches located in Lalibela, a city in the Amhara Region of Ethiopia. The church likely dates to the twelfth or thirteenth century.

name: Church of St. Gião
description: A Paleochristian sanctuary in Portugal.

name: Church of St. Peter
description: A twelfth-century AD Christian church was built atop the remains of the Roman temple of Apollo.

name: Church of the Annunziatella
description: The church of the Annunziatella is a Catholic church located in Rome's Ardeatino district. It is situated between the via Ardeatina and via di Grotta Perfetta. The church was established in the thirteenth century, as an inscription of Pope Honorius III attests.

name: Church of the Archangels at Sige
description: Church in the village of Sige (modern Kumyaka) on the southern shore of the Sea of Marmara. Constructed under Constantine VI in 780, the structure was restored twice, in 1448 and 1819. It remained in continuous use until it was ultimately abandoned in 1922.

name: Church of the Holy Apostles (Patmos)
description: 17th-century church behind the Monastery of Saint John the Theologian in Patmos.

name: Civita di Bagno
description: Civita di Bagno is the modern name for a small settlement that dates back at least to the Roman Late Republican period. Within the modern town, several key monuments are known, such as a podium temple (late 2nd c. BCE?), identified as that of Feronia by an inscription from the reign of Tiberius, a bath complex beneath Palazzo Oliva, and the medieval remains of the cathedral of San Massimo which reuses much ancient material, as well as funerary remains along the ancient road network. On the adjacent Moritola hill is a vast system of terracing, which in its earliest phase dates to the 2nd c. BCE, that supported a monumental complex of difficult interpretation, but with an important hydraulic infrastructure. This complex underwent multiple modifications over the centuries; in the 5th c. CE the complex was transformed by the installation of two kilns and a limekiln. By the 6th c. CE Civita di Bagno was the seat of a bishop.

name: Cocullo
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 44 E1 Cocullo

name: Coleshill
description: Excavations in the late 1970s revealed evidence for a multi-phase sanctuary. The Iron Age site was of circular plan, while the Romano-British site, of two phases, consisted of a walled enclosure with two shrines.

name: Colle Sparanise
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 44 G2 Colle Sparanise

name: Colonnes de Riez
description: The remains of a Roman temple of the first century CE.

name: Comblessac
description: A heptagonal Gallo-Roman temple.

name: Compitum
description: A multi-period sanctuary near Anagnia.

name: Coombe Hill
description: Coombe Hill in East Sussex is the site of a Neolithic causeway enclosure built ca. 3200 B.C.

name: Corlier
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 17 E1 Corlier

name: Corvey
description: The abbey was first founded in 815 by Charlemagne's cousins, Wala and Adelard.

name: Costa Bellene/Ballene (Capo Don?)
description: A Roman mansio on the via Iulia Augusta. An Early Christian complex (basilica, baptistery, cemetery) developed nearby and lasted until the Middle Ages.

name: Costa Murata archaeological area
description: A site with votive activity from the Archaic period and a later Roman house in addition to the basalt-paved Roman road.

name: Coste Guizon
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 15 A1 Coste Guizon

name: Cova de les Dones
description: The cave known as Cova de les Dones is located in the Millares municipality, province of Valencia, Spain. This site shows evidence of human activity across a broad period stretching from the Paleolithic to the time of the Roman Empire.

name: Crepaddosso
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 D3 Crepaddosso

name: Crypt and church of San Lussorio
description: The Romanesque church of San Lussorio dates to the early twelfth century.

name: Cueva del Agua
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 26 D3 Cueva del Agua

name: Cybele sanctuary
description: A temple sacred to Cybele located at Neuss am Rhein.

name: Cybele, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 62 A2 Cybele, T.

name: Cybele, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 62 B2 Cybele, T.

description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 62 D3 Cybele, T.

name: Cyriacus, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 89 C3 Cyriacus, Mon.

name: Dagana
description: An ancient city of southern Sri Lanka. It was characterized by Ptolemy as "sacred to Selene", a description repeated in the Arabic geographic tradition. Its precise location is uncertain: BAtlDir echoes earlier suggestions (see RE) that tentatively identify it with Tangalla, but RE would prefer as "more likely" an identification with the temple complex and associated settlement at Tenavaram (modern Tongra), an identification also suggested by earlier scholars.

name: Danielsberg
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 19 F3 Danielsberg

name: Darre-ye Barre
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 94 C4 Darre-ye Barre

name: Daventry
description: Roman building interpreted either as a villa or a shrine at Borough Hill hillfort, United Kingdom.

name: Dea Dia (temple)
description: The Arval Brethren cultivated the cult of the Dea Dia. The remains of this Augustan era temple, located at La Tavernaccia restaurant in the Magliana Vecchia neighborhood of Rome, are subterranean and not open to the public.

name: Deir Abou Makar
description: A Coptic Orthodox monastery located in the Wadi El Natrun northwest of Cairo.

name: Deir Abou Menas
description: An ancient Christian pilgrimage site that grew into an active town centered around the monastery complex devoted to Saint Menas who is said to have been buried there. The town reached its peak as the most visited pilgrimage site in Egypt during the sixth century AD.

name: Delphinion (Miletos)
description: The Hellenistic- and Roman-period sanctuary of Apollo Delphinios is located northeast of the North Market at Miletos.

name: Demeter Temple (Aegae - Yuntdağı)
description: A temple complex at Aegae-Yuntdağı, associated with the cult of Demeter and Kore based on epigraphic evidence, may also have been sacred to other divinities. The site is located to the west of the Scaenae frons of the theater complex.

name: Demeter, T.

name: Dharmaraja Cave
description: Modern name of a seventh-century Pallava cave temple in Mahābalipuram.

name: Didymoi
description: A place described by Pausanias who indicates that three sanctuaries are to be found here, namely those of Apollo, Poseidon, and Demeter.

name: Diktynnaion
description: A sanctuary located at Menies bay in Chania, Crete, with remains ranging in date from Hellenistic period to Late Antiquity.

name: Dilmun Temple at Saar
description: 20th century BCE Dilmun temple on the highest part of the ancient settlement mound at Saar in modern Bahrain.

name: Dimri

name: Dionysos Bresagenes, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 C3 Dionysos Bresagenes, T.

description: An ancient sanctuary located in Maroneia-Sapes Municipality, Greece.

name: Domburg
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 11 D1 Domburg

name: Donon
description: A sanctuary of the god Mercury in the Vosges Mountains.

name: Doric Temple at Kardaki
description: An Archaic Greek temple at Corfu built in the Doric order ca. 500 B.C.

name: Doric temple at Kaulonia
description: The Doric Temple at Caulonia has been dated ca. 420 B.C.

name: Doss Trento
description: Doss Trento is a small hill that lies along the course of the Adige River. A Paleochristian basilica was located on the hillside.

name: Dranda
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 87 G2 Dranda

name: Drion
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 45 C1 Drion

name: Duino Mithraeum
description: A Mithraeum near Duino, in the province of Trieste, north-eastern Italy, that is located within a natural cave.

name: Dún Ailinne
description: An ancient ceremonial site located in County Kildare, Ireland. The circular enclosure once covered ca. 13 ha.

name: Dur-(Kuri)galzu
description: Dur-(Kuri)galzu was founded by the Kassite ruler Kurigalzu ca. 1400 B.C. At its peak the site covered some 225 ha.

name: E-ibbi-Anum
description: The temple of the god Uraš at Dilbat. The Akkadian ceremonial name E-ibbi-Anum means "House, Anu Called It into Being."

name: E(...)gakuga
description: The shrine/seat of the god Ennugi in the Egipar temple at Ur. The name is damaged and the complete Sumerian ceremonial name of the structure is not known.

name: Eamaškuga
description: The temple of the goddess Ningal at Kissik. The Sumerian ceremonial name Eamaškuga means "House, Pure Sheepfold."

name: Eanna
description: The temple of the god Anu and the goddess Ištar at Uruk. The Sumerian ceremonial name Eanna means "House of Heaven."

name: Ebabbar
description: The temple of the god Šamaš at Sippar. The Sumerian ceremonial name Ebabbar means "Shining House."

name: Ebabbar
description: The temple of the god Šamaš at Larsa. The Sumerian ceremonial name Ebabbar means "Shining House."

name: ed-Deir
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 76 E4 ed-Deir

name: Edimgalkalama
description: The temple of the god Ištarān/Anu-rabû at Der. The Sumerian ceremonial name Edimgalkalama means "House, Great Bond of the Land."

name: Egalmah
description: The temple of the goddess Gula (as Ninisinna) at Isin. The Sumerian ceremonial name Egalmah means "Exalted Palace."

name: Église Saint-Vincent de Marimbault
description: A 12th-century CE church thought to have been built on the site of a Gallo-Roman villa. It is located in the modern French commune of Marimbault (Gironde).

name: Ehulhul
description: The temple of the god Sîn at Harran. The Sumerian ceremonial name Ehulhul means "House which Gives Joy."

name: Eigikalamma
description: The temple of the god Lugal-Marda at Marad. The Sumerian ceremonial name Eigikalamma means "House, Eye of the Land."

name: Eileithyias Antron
description: The Cave of Eileithyia outside Heraklion on Crete is an ancient sanctuary sacred to the goddess of childbirth. The shrine was active during Neolithic, Minoan, and Mycenaean phases.

name: El Bovalar
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 F4 El Bovalar

name: el Hofra
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 31 F4 el Hofra

name: el-Chatatba

name: Elephant tomb at Carmona
description: Structure in the the necropolis of Carmona (near Seville) named for a small sculpture of an elephant found inside it. Rather than a tomb, it may have been a sanctuary or temple.

name: Elephanta Caves
description: An island in the Sea of Oman is home to the "City of Caves", where an important assemblage of rock-cut art dating to the fifth through eighth centuries and linked to the cult of Shiva may be found.

name: Eleusinioi Theoi, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 E4 Eleusinioi Theoi, T.

name: Ellès
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 33 D1 Ellès

name: Elst
description: In the village of Elst, located between Arnhem and Nijmegen, the remains of two quadrangular Romano-Celtic-type temples were discovered beneath the parish church following World War II.

name: Emecik
description: An ancient settlement and sanctuary site on the Cnidus (modern Reşadiye/Datça) peninsula.

name: Emeslam
description: The temple of the god Nergal at Cutha. The Sumerian ceremonial name Emeslam means "House, Warrior of the Netherworld."

name: Enamhe
description: The temple of the gods Adad and Apla-Adad at Anat (Anatho). The Sumerian ceremonial name Enamhe means "House of Plenty."

name: Epaone
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 17 D3 Epaone

name: Epiais-Rhus
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 11 C3 Epiais-Rhus

name: Episcopal complex of Eio-el Tolmo de Minateda (Héllín, Albacete, Spain)
description: The episcopal complex of Eio at El Tolmo de Minateda dates to the terminal sixth century CE and was in use during the following two centuries. Its construction may have been directed by Visigothic officials in Toletum.

name: Epoissum
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 11 F3 Epoissum

name: Erebe
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 E2 Erebe

name: Eremo di San Michele (Coli)
description: The cavern of San Michele was a hermit's dwelling place that is located near a cavern known as the "cave of San Colombano". This hermitage lies in a relatively solitary area of the valley of the Curiasca di San Michele stream, a tributary of the Trebbia river (Italian municipality of Coli, province of Piacenza).

name: Eremo di San Michele Arcangelo
description: A rock-cut Christian sanctuary located on Monte Altino in the Monti Aurunci that was begun ca. A.D. 830.

name: Ermita de S. Valero
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 F4 Ermita de S. Valero

name: es-Soumaa
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 31 F4 es-Soumaa

name: Ešahula
description: The temple of the god Nergal at Me-Turnat. The Sumerian ceremonial name Ešahula means "House of the Happy Heart."

name: Esterzili
description: The Domu de Orgia at Esterzili is the largest nuragic "megaron" structure thus far discovered in Sardinia.

name: Estrées-St-Denis
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 11 C3 Estrées-St-Denis

name: Etchmiadzin Cathedral
description: A fourth century A.D. church located in Ejmiatsin, Armenia. This is the central cathedral of the Mother See of Holy Etchmiadzin of the Armenian Apostolic Church and was enrolled as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2000.

name: Etruscan acropolis at Volaterrae
description: The multi-phase acropolis of Etruscan Volaterrae has numerous important phases, beginning in the Archaic period. The monumental site continued to develop and grow during the Hellenistic period. It was identified archaeologically only in the nineteenth century. In the twentieth century it was the subject of major investigations under M. Cristofani and, later, M. Bonamici.

name: Etruscan altar of Grotta Porcina
description: A rock-cut altar dating to the first half of the sixth century B.C.

name: Etruscan sanctuary of San Lorenzo Nuovo
description: An Etruscan sanctuary located on Monte Landro northwest of Lake Bolsena.

name: Eulmaš
description: The temple of the goddess Anunnītu at Sippar-Anunnītu (=Sippar-Amnanum).

name: Euphrasian Basilica
description: An Early Byzantine Christian basilica located in Poreč, Croatia.

name: Eurmeiminanki
description: The ziggurat of the god the god Nabû at Borsippa. The Sumerian ceremonial name Eurmeiminanki means "House which Gathers the Seven (Me's) of Heaven and Underworld."

name: Fanum Pithiviers-le-Vieil
description: The Fanum of Pithiviers-le-Vieil.

name: Fanum Voltumnae
description: A shrine of Voltumna that served as the chief religious center of Etruria. The Etruscan League (duodecim populi Etruriae) was said to meet annually at the shrine. Archaeological excavations of the site of Campo della Fiera have been posited as the definitive location of the Fanum Voltumnae.

name: Faras Cathedral
description: The cathedral in Faras, founded in 620 and rebuilt in the 8th and 10th centuries, respectively, was the seat of the Diocese of Faras. The site had well-preserved wall paintings from the 8th to 9th centuries. The cathedral was flooded by the creation of Lake Nasser in the 1960s.

name: Farfa Abbey
description: A Benedictine abbey located ca. 60 km from Rome.

name: Farley Heath

name: Filitosa
description: Filitosa is a megalithic site in southern Corsica that was in use from the Neolithic period until Roman times. A number of menhirs have been discovered at the site.

name: Fines
description: A Gallo-Roman site lying along the Roman road from Lyon to Saintes.

name: Fiume di Mallo
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 47 E4 Fiume di Mallo

name: Flag Fen
description: Flag Fen is a Bronze Age ritual site near Peterborough, England, comprised of 60,000 timbers arranged in five rows that connect Whittlesey Island with Peterborough. Dendrochronological evidence has dates the site to ca. 1365–967 BC.

name: Fluy
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 11 C3 Fluy

name: Fons Clitumni
description: Originally the site of a shrine dedicated to the river god Clitumnus and later a paleochristian shrine. The temple was listed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2011.

name: Fontans
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 17 A4 Fontans

name: Fonte Romito
description: A pre-Roman and Roman settlement of Samnium.

name: Fontecchio

description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 19 C2 Forggensee

description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 81 B3 Forum Cambusis?

name: Forum Cassii
