name: Fountain of Anna Perenna
description: Sacred to the goddess Anna Perenna and the nymphs, this rectangular fountain surrounded by rupestral inscriptions was excavated on the modern Via Guidubaldo del Monte in Rome's Pinciano neighborhood during the late 20th century. Rich votive deposits complement the picture of the goddess' worship painted by Ovid in the Fasti entry for the Ides of March.

name: Fox-Amphoux
description: At Fox-Amphoux the remains of a Roman villa were discovered, adjacent to those of a sanctuary ad fines that may have been dedicated to the Capitoline triad. The site was in use from the time of Augustus until the third century AD.

name: Francavilla di Sicilia
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 47 G3 Francavilla di Sicilia

name: Frascole, S. Martino a Poggio
description: A stips votiva was discovered at the site of the Christian church of San Martino a Poggio - Frascole in 1887. The main ancient activity at the site likely took place during the middle of the third century B.C.

name: Freienbach
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 19 A2 Freienbach

name: Friars Wash Roman Temple
description: The Friars Wash Roman Temple was noted first in 1965. Aerial photography and subsequent archaeological investigation identified a system of ditches and two rectangular structures that were considered to be Romano-British temples.

name: Frontone
description: An Umbro-Roman temple of Iuppiter on the slopes of Monte Catria.

name: Funerary temple of Seti I
description: Temple dedicated to the cult of King Seti I located on the west bank of Thebes.

name: Furfo
description: A vicus of the Vestini located in the upper Aterno valley in Abruzzo. The ancient settlement was located some 15 km south of modern L'Aquila, in the vicinity of S. Maria di Forfona near Barisciano. Furfo lay on the Via Claudia Nova between Amiternum and Peltuinum.

name: Furzol
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 69 C2 Furzol

name: Gabrias
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 17 A4 Gabrias

name: Gallarus Oratory
description: An early Christian church in Ireland, originally built between the sixth and the ninth centuries.

name: Gallo-Roman fanum at Lapanouse-de-Cernon
description: The remains of a possible Gallo-Roman fanum were recovered, including an enclosure surrounding a rectangular structure along with terra sigillata from La Graufesenque.

name: Gallo-Roman fanum at Recoules-Prévinquières
description: The fanum of a Gallo-Roman cult that was in use from the first century B.C. to the fourth century A.D.

name: Gallo-Roman remains at La Tour, Sablé-sur-Sarthe
description: The remains of a Gallo-Roman sanctuary.

name: Gallo-Roman Sanctuary at Bû
description: Remains of a rectangular Romano-Celtic temple were excavated north of the French town of Bû (in the department of Eure-et-Loir), beginning in the 1960s. Other buildings of uncertain nature and a theater were also identified in the vicinity. Coins and pottery discovered at the site indicate there was activity there during the Roman imperial period.

name: Gallo-Roman Sanctuary at Hanches
description: A Gallo-Roman sanctuary identified in the 1960s at La Cavée du Moulin near Hanches in the Eure-et-Loir department of France.

name: Gallo-Roman sites at Érome, France
description: A pair of archaeological sites in Gallia Narbonensis. The site of Les Orpaillots shows traces of a sanctuary and a group of tombs while Notre-Dame-de-la-Mure showed traces of a late third century Gallo-Roman villa.

name: Gallo-Roman temple at Nasium
description: Archaeological remains of the Gallo-Roman temple at ancient Nasium, located at Mazeroie, commune Naix-aux-Forges

name: Gallo-Roman temple at Vannes
description: Remains of a Gallo-Roman at Vannes.

name: Gallo-Roman temple of Forêt d'Halatte (cne. Ognon, Oise)
description: A Gallo-Roman temple built ca. mid-first century AD that remained in use until the fifth century.

name: Gamaches-en-Vexin
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 11 B3 Gamaches-en-Vexin

name: Gandzasar
description: 13th century Armenian cathedral located near the village of Vank in the disputed region of Nagorno-Karabakh/Artsakh.

name: Gantiadi
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 87 F1 Gantiadi

name: Garian
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 35 F2 Garian

name: Garizein (mountain)
description: One of two mountains in the immediate vicinity of the West Bank city of Nablus.

name: Gasr ar-Rakam
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 38 D1 Gasr ar-Rakam

name: Gasr Bu Zayd
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 38 D1 Gasr Bu Zayd

name: Gasr el-Gatres
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 38 B1 Gasr el-Gatres

name: Gasr el-Gezira
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 35 E2 Gasr el-Gezira

name: Gasr Silu
description: Gasr Silu, Libya, is an ancient site documented by the British School at Rome under John Bryan Ward-Perkins in 1969. Field study was conducted by Sheila Gibson and Margaret Browne. Photographs in the BSR archive document architectural remains at the site that include productive installations and the remains of two Byzantine churches. A Byzantine fort (gasr) was also identified.

name: Gasr Stablous
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 38 C1 no. 13 (Gasr Stablous)

name: Gasr Zeitun
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 38 D1 Gasr Zeitun

name: Gastaldaga area and the Episcopal complex
description: A Longobard complex in Cividale del Friuli.

name: Gebel Tafnis
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 79 D4 Gebel Tafnis

name: Gebel Teir
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 79 D2 Gebel Teir

name: Gelati Monastery
description: The Monastery of Gelati was founded in 1106 in western Georgia. It was inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1984.

name: Geran
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 35 F2 Geran

name: Gerasimos
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 70 H2 Gerasimos

name: Germeshin
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 79 D4 Germeshin

name: Geronisos
description: A small island lying off the west coast of Cyprus where a sanctuary has been identified.

name: Geth Rabbi
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 76 E4 Geth Rabbi

name: Ġgantija
description: Megalithic temple on the island of Gozo; named a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1980

name: Ghulbiyan
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 98 F3 Ghulbiyan

name: Giado
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 35 E3 Giado

name: Gialia Monastery
description: The Gialia Monastery is a ruined medieval Georgian Orthodox monastery located in the village of Gialia, Paphos District, northwestern Cyprus.

name: Gilan Gharbi
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 92 A2 Gilan Gharbi

name: Giza
description: The site of Giza encompasses the Giza Necropolis, a large archaeological site famous for its pyramid complexes and monumental sculpture.

name: Glak, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 89 D2 Glak, Mon.

name: Glale
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 89 E3 Glale

name: Glava Panega
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 22 B5 Glava Panega

name: Göbekli Tepe
description: The site of Göbekli Tepe, located in the Germuş mountains of south-eastern Anatolia, was the location of a Neolithic hilltop sanctuary. This sanctuary, thought to be a series of stone enclosures with T-shaped pillars carrying carving of wild animals, was built in the Pre-Pottery Neolithic and ranges in date from 9,600 to 8,200 B.C.

name: Gola di S. Andrea, Itri
description: Located along the course of the Via Appia between Fondi and Itri are the remains of a likely sanctuary of Apollo that dates to the fourth and third century BCE.

name: Golyamo Belovo
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 22 B6 Golyamo Belovo

name: Görresburg
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 11 G2 Görresburg

name: Görtschach
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 19 E3 Görtschach

name: Gosbecks
description: The Gosbecks farm site is the location of a Romano-Celtic temple. The shrine measures 100 meters square and a pre-Roman enclosure lies within the later temenos. The pre-Roman site was likely sacred to the war-god Camulos. Camulos was later synchronized with the Roman god Mars, although evidence for the worship of Mercury was also discovered at the site.

name: Gournay-sur-Aronde
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 11 C3 Gournay-sur-Aronde

name: Grattauli
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 47 C3 Grattauli

name: Great Temple of Hera
description: The Great Temple of Hera, or Polycrates Temple, is the third temple constructed at the Heraion of Samos between 540 and 530 BCE.

name: Great Temple of Petra
description: The Great Temple of Petra is one of the major archaeological and architectural components of central Petra. It is located to the south of the Colonnaded Street and southeast of the Temenos Gate and covers 7560 square meters.

name: Greek Temple at Taras
description: A Doric temple dating to the sixth century B.C., located in the modern piazza Castello in Taranto, Italy. It was initially attributed to Poseidon, but may have been dedicated to a different deity.

name: Gremoulias ancient sanctuary
description: A sanctuary -- with remains of at least two successive temples (6th C BCE and 4th C BCE), a great altar, and votive deposits -- located on the modern Gremoulias saddle, 3.5km SE of Kalavryta in the northern Peloponnese. The site is not known from ancient literature. Scholars speculate that the deity worshiped may have been Artemis and the site may have been an extra-urban sanctuary of nearby Kynaitha.

name: Grotta Trinità
description: The Grotta Trinità is a cave sanctuary located in the comune of Ruffano, province of Lecce, in southern Italy. The cave was in use from the Neolithic period onwards. In the Iron Age, terracotta vessels with incised graffiti date activity to the sixth century BCE and subsequent.

name: Grotto of the Sibyl
description: A cave discovered by Amedeo Maiuri in 1932 and connected in the historical tradition with the Cumaean Sibyl. The site features in the sixth book of Virgil's Aeneid.

name: Güglingen Mithraeum
description: A sanctuary dedicated to Mithras located near modern Heilbronn, Germany, in use during the second-third centuries CE.

name: Gur-i Dukhtar
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 94 B4 Gur-i Dukhtar

name: Gur-i Shapur
description: The so-called "Shapur cave" in the Zagros Mountains contains a colossal statue of Shapur I, the second ruler of the Sasanid Empire.

name: Gura Dobrogei
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 22 F4 Gura Dobrogei

name: Gurzuf
description: According to Hind, a sanctuary dating ca. third century BC to second century AD was identified at the Gurzuf Pass. Silver and bronze statuettes of Graeco-Roman divinities were recovered.

name: Guzana (Tell Halaf)
description: An Aramaean principality and Neo-Assyrian provincial capital founded on the site of an extinct Neolithic settlement. Settled through Hellenistic times, it was abandoned at the end of the first century BCE.

name: Gyroulas
description: A Late Archaic rural sanctuary of Demeter and Apollo at Gyroulas on Naxos.

name: Hagar Qin Megalithic Temple Complex (Malta)
description: Ħaġar Qim is a megalithic temple complex (ca. 3600-3200 BC), included in the UNESCO World Heritage List.

name: Hagia Sophia, Iznik
description: Hagia Sophia, Iznik, Turkey, was founded as a Christian basilica in the fourth century CE. Justinian I rebuilt the site in the mid-6th century CE. An earthquake in the eleventh century destroyed that church, and it was then substantially rebuilt following 1065. It is to that rebuilding that much of the present structure pertains. Following the Ottoman conquest, the church became a mosque in 1331. The site served that function until ca. 1935, when it became a museum. Following 2011, it again served as a mosque.

name: Hala Sultan Tekke
description: A major Late Bronze Age settlement occupied the site of Hala Sultan Tekke, which later became an important Sufi Islamic shrine associated with Umm Haram.

name: Halberg Mithraeum
description: A natural cave in the side of the Halberg hill at Saarbrücken was enlarged for the use of the Mithras cult.

name: Harlow
description: Harlow is the site of a pre-Roman and Roman sanctuary.

name: Harran el-Awamid
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 69 E3 Harran el-Awamid

name: Hasanaliler Basilica
description: Hasanaliler Church is a 6th-century Byzantine church.

name: Hathor Temple, Timna Valley
description: A shrine sacred to Hathor connected with the Egyptian mining settlement that was extracting copper in the Timna Valley.

name: Haut Bécherel
description: Haut Bécherel is the site of a major Gallo-Roman sanctuary sacred to Mars.

name: Hayling Island Iron-Age/Roman Sanctuary
description: Modern Hayling Island (borough of Havant, Hampshire, England) is the site of a multi-phase sanctuary: the Iron Age shrine dates ca. 50 BCE and the Roman temple dates ca. 55-60CE. The sanctuary seems to have suffered decline and ceased being maintained in the early third century CE, although ritual/votive deposition seems to have continued through the fourth century.

name: Hecate, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 53 B2 Hecate, T.

name: Hekateion
description: The Hekateion is a monumental temple for the goddess Hekate constructed in Lagina ca. 125 BCE.

name: Henchir Krebita
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 35 B1 Henchir Krebita

name: Henchir Oum Iala
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 34 F3 Henchir Oum Iala

name: Henchir Snem
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 35 C2 Henchir Snem

name: Henley Wood
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 8 E3 Henley Wood

name: Hephaistia
description: An ancient settlement on the northern coast of the Greek island of Lemnos.

name: Hephaistion
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 65 D5 Hephaistion

name: Hera temple
description: The so-called 'Tavole Palatine' is the site of the ruins of a sixth century B.C. Doric temple sacred to Hera and associated with the city of Metapontum.

name: Hera, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 B3 Hera, T.

name: Heraion
description: The Heraion of Perachora.

name: Heraion (Argolis)
description: The Argive Heraion was a major ancient sanctuary sacred to Hera located in the Argolid.

name: Herakleion
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 59 C2 Herakleion

name: Herakles, T.
description: A sanctuary of Herakles located on the island of Lemnos, active from the Archaic period through to the time of the Roman empire.

name: Hermai
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 58 D3 Hermai

name: Hermel
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 68 B5 Hermel

name: Hermes and Aphrodite Sanctuary
description: This large, open-air sanctuary near Syme Viannou in southern Crete was in use from 2000 BCE until at least the sixth century CE. It attracted pilgrims who are assumed to have camped at the site, which is far from any contemporary settlement. Monumental construction began in the early second millennium BCE and multiple phases and changes are evident, including the construction of Christian chapels in late antiquity. Although the identities of the deities worshiped here at the earliest periods are uncertain (as are the saints venerated in the Christian period), the association with Hermes and Aphrodite seems to have begun as early as the 10th century BCE.

name: Heroon of Eretria
description: A triangular enclosure with approximately 19 burials (both cremation and inhumation) dating to the Geometric period (ca. 750 BC - ca. 630 BC).

name: Heroon of Trysa
description: The Heroon of Trysa is a scholarly appellation applied to an ancient tomb (ca. 380 BCE) found at Trysa in ancient Lycia. Julius August Schönborn discovered the site in 1841. The shrine is noted for its cycle of relief sculpture.

name: Hexapyla
description: A sanctuary located north of Syracuse.

name: Hibbariye
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 69 C3 Hibbariye

name: Hieron (Byzantion)
description: A sanctuary that was sacred to Poseidon and Zeus Ourios located at the Black Sea mouth.

name: Hlahlah, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 89 E3 Hlahlah, Mon.

name: Hochscheid
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 11 H3 Hochscheid

name: Hockwold
description: A Romano-Celtic temple site dating between the second and fourth centuries AD.

name: Horeb Mons, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 76 D4 Horeb Mons, Mon.

name: Horvat 'Erav
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 69 B3 Horvat 'Erav

name: Horvat Shema'
description: Ancient synagogue site and burial place of Rabbi Shamai just south of Meron, Israel. The site was occupied in the early 1st century BCE, again from the late 2nd to the late 3rd, from the early 4th century to the mid-7th century, from this point until the mid-9th century, and finally from the mid-12th century until the late-13th century.

name: Hosion kai Dikaion, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 62 C3 Hosion kai Dikaion, T.

name: Hosios Loukas
description: A tenth-century walled monastery in Boeotia, Greece.

name: Husn Niha
description: Husn Niha is the site of four Roman temples that were constructed between the first and third centuries AD.

name: Husn Sfiri
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 68 B5 Husn Sfiri

name: Hyperteleaton
description: Sanctuary of Apollo Hyperteleatas, partially excavated in 1885 and 1968. In ancient literature the sanctuary is only mentioned by Pausanias, in whose time it was called the Hyperteleaton and was dedicated to Asclepius. The site is located south of Phoiniki in the southeastern Peloponnese at a place known in the 19th century called Chasanaga.

name: Hypogeum of Ħal-Saflieni (Malta)
description: A prehistoric, subterranean temple; named as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1980.

name: Hyria (Naxos)
description: A sixth century B.C. sanctuary located on the island of Naxos.

name: Hyrkania/Kastellion
description: Hyrkania was an ancient fortress in the Judaean Dessert that was rebuilt as the Byzantine monastery of Kastellion (ca. A.D 492).

name: I Cappuccini
description: The church and convent "I Cappuccini" in Frascati was the site of a Roman villa.

name: Idaion Antron
description: A sacred cave on Crete where it was believed Rhea hid Zeus to protect him from Cronus. Archaeological evidence indicates use as a shelter or dwelling as early as the Late Neolithic period, with religious activities taking over in the Bronze Age and continuing through the Roman period.

name: Idomene Minor
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 54 D3 Idomene Minor

name: Ilıca
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 62 D3 Ilıca

name: Imst
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 19 C2 Imst

name: Industria/Bodincomagum?
description: Industria/Bodincomagum? (Monteu da Po) was a Ligurian settlement in the Po valley. Under Roman influence the site became known as Industria, a colonial establishment, in regio IX of the Augustan organization of Italy. The colony is likely founded ca. 124 B.C.

name: Iohannes Urtius, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 89 C3 Iohannes Urtius, Mon.

name: Ionaion
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 58 unlocated Ionaion

name: Iovis Tifatinus
description: A sanctuary sacred to Jupiter located on the western slopes of Tifata Mons (Monte Tifata).

name: Irru
description: A nuragic shrine with a sacred well.

name: Is Pirois
description: A sacred well sanctuary, Pozzo sacro Is Pirois, is associated with the Nuragic culture. Is Pirois is located in the municipality of Villaputzu, in metropolitan Cagliari. The well is built of schist, and the covering structure is a pseudo-dome or tholos.

name: Ishkhani (monastery)
description: A church and monastery in Tao-Klarjeti, first constructed in the 7th century.

name: Ishtar Temple at Mari
description: Temple dedicated to the goddess Ishtar located in the western section of the city of Mari.

name: Isieiou Oros
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 80 B5/ inset Isieiou Oros

name: Isola del Tinetto
description: An island in the Ligurian Sea.

name: Italic temple of Castel di Ieri
description: The remains of an Italic temple, in the locality known as "Pié di Francia". The sanctuary dates from the fourth to first centuries B.C.

name: Itea
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 C4 Itea

name: Iton?
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 D2 Iton?

name: Iuktas
description: A Minoan peak sanctuary on Juktas that served the Minoan palace of Knossos. The site was first excavated in 1909 by Sir Arthur Evans. The site was in use from Early to Late Bronze Age.

name: Iunonis Curritis Lucus
description: The sacred 'Grove of Iuno Curritis' located in Falerii Veteres.

name: Iuppiter Latiaris, T.
description: An important Latial sanctuary of Iuppiter located on Mons Albanus (modern Monte Cavo).

name: Izernore
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 18 C3 Izernore

name: Jandial Temple
description: Jandial is the site of a semi-Classical temple of Ionic Greek design constructed in the first or second centuries BCE. It is the farthest east of any known Hellenic temple.

name: Jebel Moneijah
description: Jebel Moneijah was the site of a Nabatean sanctuary in the Southern Sinai.

name: Jebel Serbal
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 76 D4 Jebel Serbal

name: Jmr-t
description: An ancient settlement, centered on the temple to Amun Nakht, located at modern Ain Birbiyeh/Birbibya, in Egypt's Dakhleh Oasis.

name: Jordan Hill Roman Temple
description: A Romano-Celtic temple dating probably to the fourth century CE. Its ancient name is unknown. The name of the hill on which it sits is spelled "Jordon" in some twentieth-century works, including the Barrington Atlas.

name: Jouima
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 28 C2 Jouima

name: Jvari
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 88 C3 Jvari

name: Kabaköy
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 87 E4 Kabaköy

name: Kabeireion
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 A2 Kabeireion

name: Kabeirion
description: A sanctuary near Thebes where the mystery cult of the god Kabeiros and his son, Pais, was celebrated.

name: Kafr Aqareb
description: An ancient place, located in modern Syria, with remains of Roman praetorium, a temple and grottoes for pagan worship, a Byzantine basilica, and a large communal reservoir. Inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2011 as part of the "Ancient Villages of Northern Syria."

name: Kai Ka'us
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 93 G3 Kai Ka'us

name: Kalabak
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 62 D2 Kalabak

name: Kalabaka
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 B1 Kalabaka

name: Kalecik
description: Kalecik (formerly known as Aralesk or Lesk) is a modern village situated ca. 5 km north of Van Kalesi in southeast Turkey. In the village church, a re-used cylindrical stone (presumably a column base) with a building inscription of the Urartian king Išpuini, son of Sarduri I (ninth century BCE), was found. Furthermore, a re-used stone slab with an inscription of the Urartian king Argišti, son of Minua and grandson of Išpuini (eighth century BCE) has also been found here.

name: Kalogeros
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 54 inset Kalogeros

name: Kalota
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 67 D4 Kalota

name: Kamul (monastery)
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 89 E3 Kamul, Mon.

name: Kapsalaes
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 C3 Kapsalaes

name: Karnasion
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 58 B3 Karnasion

name: Karthaia, Athena Temple
description: A Doric-order temple, hexastyle in antis (23.20x11.10 m). The building utilizes stone from both Aegina and Paros.

name: Kasnakovo
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 22 C6 Kasnakovo

name: Kastri Livadiou ancient settlement
description: An archaeological site located 4.5km west southwest of modern Dolichi in Greece's Larisa regional unit (Thessaly). Here, a proto-byzantine walled settlement with three basilicas was built partly with spolia from a temple of Herakles and the ancient city of Doliche. On this basis, the excavators have argued for identification of the archaeology with the attested name; however, the lack so far of in-situ evidence predating the 6th century CE has led other scholars to continue to identify the original Classical-Roman polis of Doliche with the archaeological site at Sarandoporo.

name: Kastro ano Diakopto
description: Abundant spolia, artifacts, and building foundations provide evidence for an ancient settlement in the area of modern Kastro ano Diakopto and the church of Ag. Athanasios on the hill above it to the west. It is probably to be identified with ancient Boura, an attested settlement of Achaia.

name: Kástron Khóstion
description: A complex of structures dating to the Greek and Roman period, with ceramic evidence of prior occupation of the vicinity, on a spur of Palióvouno, overlooking a valley leading to the bay of Saránda on the Korinthian Gulf. The excavators associate this site with the attested classical city of Khorsiai on the basis of epigraphic evidence recovered at the site.

name: Kazerun
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 94 B4 Kazerun

name: Kelisaye Espakho
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 97 B2 Kelisaye Espakho

name: Kenaion
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 D3 Kenaion

name: Kephalos Island Settlement
description: On the small island of modern Kephalos in the Ambracian Gulf, archaeologists discovered a settlement, a pair of Christian basilicas, and other associated structures and small finds. The site may have begun as a refuge settlement in the late fifth / early sixth century CE and seems to have continued in use, possibly with a late monastic function, into the 10th century.

name: Kepia
description: The sanctuary of Heros Auloneites at Kepia.

name: Kerlescan alignments
description: A complex of 555 megaliths arranged in 13 rows.

name: Khane Gauhar
description: Remains of a Buddhist shrine of uncertain date located near modern Darwishan in the Nawa-i-Barakzayi district of Afghanistan's Helmand province.

name: Khayrabad Bala
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 93 G3 Khayrabad Bala

name: Kherba de Sfisifa
description: A rural location now in the modern Algerian municipal territory of Djouab (Médéa province) where Gsell and his correspondents (AAA, whence BAtlas) observed Roman ruins including a large building with columns that G. speculated might have been a church.

name: Khirbat al-Mudayna
description: A small Moabite sanctuary within a walled-in enclosure was discovered on the Wadi ath-Thamad in Jordan.

name: Khirbet ed Deir
description: The early Byzantine monastery located at Khirbet ed-Deir in the Judean desert was excavated from 1981-1987 by Yizhar Hirschfeld. The remote site yielded important evidence about monastic life from the fourth to seventh centuries CE.

name: Khirbet el Quneitira
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 70 G3 Khirbet el Quneitira

name: Khirbet el-Bilaas
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 68 E4 Khirbet el-Bilaas

name: Khirbet el-Knese
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 69 C2 Khirbet el-Knese

name: Khirbet et-Tannur
description: Located some 70 km from Petra, Khirbet et-Tannur is the site of a well-preserved, open-air Nabataean sanctuary.

name: Khirbet Jedora
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 69 B4 Khirbet Jedora

name: Khirbet Semrin
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 68 E4 Khirbet Semrin

name: Khondro Teikhos
description: A Classical-period sanctuary at Khondro Teikhos, "said to be somewhere NE of Glossa in Skopelos, Sporades, Magnesia, Thessaly" (according to ToposText).

name: Khontro Bigli
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 C3 Khontro Bigli

name: Khortata
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 54 C4 Khortata

name: Khrysomilia
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 C2 Khrysomilia

name: Khrysovitsa
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 B3 Khrysovitsa

name: Khurha
description: A partially-excavated complex near modern Qum in Iran. The site was possibly a sanctuary and likely dates to the Seleucid age. There is some evidence that the site continued in use into the Parthian period.

name: Khurramdasht
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 92 G3 Khurramdasht

name: Khwaja Ali Sehyaka/Sehyak
description: This "Mesopotamian-style temple" site located near modern Dishu in Afghanistan's Helmand province was excavated in the 1970s. Carbon-14 dates and "Greek-style architectural fragments and fine red pottery" led the excavators to date the use of the site from the late Hellenistic and Parthian period to "the beginning of the Sasanian period in the 3rd century" CE. Finds included a fragmentary bilingual (Greek/Aramaic) inscription possibly mentioning a Parthian king Phraates and the Iranian deity Ahuramazda.

name: Kiboreia
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 69 D2 Kiboreia

name: Kiratah
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 94 C5 Kiratah

name: Kirche auf der Akropolis
description: A Byzantine church located within the fortified acropolis of Aperlae.

name: Kition
description: Kition was an ancient city founded on the southern coast of Cyprus in the thirteenth century B.C.

name: Klaros
description: The site of a temple of Apollo administered by Colophonians and active from the seventh century B.C. to the fourth century A.D.

name: Kleonai
description: An ancient city located in the mountainous area southwest of Corinth. The acropolis of ancient Kleonai lies about two kilometers east of the modern town named after it in the 1960s (Archaies Kleones, formerly Kontostavlos).

name: Knowlton Church and Earth Works
description: A cluster of Neolithic and Bronze Age earthworks with a twelfth-century Norman church built in the center.

name: Kobosh
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 81 C2 Kobosh

name: Koçak
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 62 D3 Koçak

name: Koh Ker
description: An ancient temple complex of the Khmer Empire, Koh Ker is also known as Lingapura or Chok Gargyar. The site is located in the Srayong commune of Preah, Vihear province's Kulen district. The site was inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage site in September 2023 and covers some 1,187 ha.

name: Koi Krylgan Kala
description: Koi Krylgan Kala is an archaeological site in Uzbekistan. At the site are the remains of a temple complex associated with the Chorasmian Dynasty, which was destroyed approximately 200 BC.

name: Kolona
description: An archaeological site on the island of Aegina that shows multiple settlement phases from the Bronze Age to the Classical period.

name: Kom el-Nana
description: Area south of the main city of Amarna that was the site of a temple during the Amarna period. The structure was overbuilt by a Christian monastery in late antiquity.

name: Komissariato
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 C3 Komissariato

name: Komosarue
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 87 M2 Komosarue

name: Korykion Antron (Parnassos)
description: A sacred cave on mount Parnassos named for Apollo's daughter Corycia. It is not to be confused with another cave of the same name at Corycus in Cilicia.

name: Köşkerli
description: Modern name for ruins of a Byzantine church and surrounding village in eastern Rough Cilicia

name: Kosmas kai Damianos, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 53 A2 Kosmas kai Damianos, Mon.

name: Kothon
description: The so-called "Kothon" is a large, rectangular, artificial pool dating to the Punic period of Sicily and located on the island of Motya. Originally interpreted as an artificial port basin, recent research reinterpets it as a freshwater basin with functions sacred to the god Ba'al and constituting the central component of a large sanctuary.

name: Koustilya
description: A late-antique rural church now lacking decoration located about 6km north of the modern village of Tozeur in Tunisia.

name: Kranaion Antron
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 60 C2 Kranaion Antron

name: Kraneia?
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 54 D3 Kraneia?

name: Krounoi

name: Kuh-i Ayyub
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 94 C3 Kuh-i Ayyub

name: Kuh-i Shahrak
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 94 C3 Kuh-i Shahrak

name: Kunar Siah
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 94 C5 Kunar Siah

name: Kuntillat 'Ajrud
description: A late ninth to early eighth century BC site located in the northeastern part of the Sinai peninsula, the fortress-like Kuntillat ‘Ajrud may have been a sanctuary. Hebrew and Phoenician inscriptions have been identified at the site.

name: Kursi paleochristian basilica
description: An early Christian basilica was excavated at Kursi, near the Sea of Galilee in the Golan Heights.

name: Kushk Khainfar
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 92 B3 Kushk Khainfar

name: L’Acapte
description: Site of a rural sanctuary dedicated to Aristaeus, son of Apollo.

name: La Bauche
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 17 F3 La Bauche

name: La Cuma, Monte Rinaldo
description: The principal archaeological remains found in the "La Cuma, Monte Rinaldo" site are those of a late Republican sanctuary dating to the third through first centuries B.C.

name: La Motte-St-Martin
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 17 F4 La Motte-St-Martin

name: La Temple de la Madeleine, Geneva
description: The oldest Christian church in the city of Geneva, the Temple de la Madeleine site has been in continuous use as a church since the 5th century. The current structure on the site dates to the 15th century.

name: Labraunda
description: Labraunda is an archaeological site located in Caria, just west of Ortaköy, Turkey. The site was sacred to both Carians and Mysians.

name: Labyrinthos
description: Labyrinthos is a name applied by Greek authors, notably Herodotus and Strabo, to describe a monumental funerary temple at Hawara.

name: Lachau
description: A Gallo-Roman sanctuary.

name: Lamyatt
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 8 E3 Lamyatt

name: Laneia
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 B3 Laneia

name: Lang-i Rud
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 92 G2 Lang-i Rud

name: Large Temple at Sikait
description: A large rock-cut temple at Sikait in Egypt's Eastern desert.

name: Laubendorf
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 19 F3 Laubendorf

name: Laveyron
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 17 D3 Laveyron

name: Laxia tis Skhistis
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 B2 Laxia tis Skhistis

name: Le Bouchet-St-Nicolas
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 17 B4 Le Bouchet-St-Nicolas

name: Le Ciliegeta
description: The site of Le Ciliegeta, near the source of the river Arno, served as a rich stips votiva. The site was discovered in 1836. The finds included over 600 bronzes, more than 1,000 pieces of aes rude, 2,000 weapons, and other artifacts.

name: Le Pinnacle
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 7 D2 Le Pinnacle

name: Le Serre d’Astier
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 17 F5 Le Serre d’Astier

name: Lehun
description: An ancient site located on the northern plateau of the Wadi Mujib in modern Jordan. Under excavation since the 1970s, the site has so far provided evidence for human activity from the prehistoric period through the Islamic.

name: Lendorf
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 19 F3 Lendorf

name: Lentilly
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 17 D2 Lentilly

name: Lera
description: An ancient cave sanctuary on the Akrotiri peninsula of Crete.

name: Les Courents
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 D2 Les Courents

name: Les Vaux de la Celle
description: An ancient Gallo-Roman sanctuary near modern Genainville (France), with excavated remains of a temple, nymphaeum, and theater: BAtlas 11 B3 Génainville (sic)

name: Letoon
description: Sanctuary of Leto and center of the Lycian federation.

name: Leuke Akte
description: Leuke Akte, the ‘White cape’ located on the Libyan coast of Egypt, was the site of an Isis cult wherein the Egyptian goddess was worshipped as Aphrodite, Muchis, and Eseremphis. An oracular shrine of Apollo was also found there.

name: Leukothea
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 88 B3 Leukothea

name: Limani Ozia
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 54 B3 Limani Ozia

name: Lincietti
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 45 B1 Lincietti

name: Lloret
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 H4 Lloret

name: Locoteiacus
description: A Benedictine abbey that is located 7 km south of Poitiers and was founded by Martin from 361 to 371.

name: Los Cuadriales
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 26 D3 Los Cuadriales

name: Lousoi
description: A settlement in Azania, located near modern Kato Lusi, that has been under excavation since the late 19th century.

name: Loutra Hypatis
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 C3 Loutra Hypatis

name: Lower Church at Banganarti
description: Church dating to the 7th century CE in Nubia that was succeeded by St. Raphael Church II. The site is located in the modern Sudanese village of Banganarti.

name: Lucus Feroniae
description: A sanctuary of Feronia located at a spring on the via Appia near Tarracina.

name: Luxeuil Abbey
description: Located in France's Burgundy region, the Luxeuil Abbey was founded ca. 585–590 by the Irish missionary Saint Columbanus.

name: Lydney
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 8 E3 Lydney

name: Lykosoura
description: A city of Arcadia and home of the cult of Despoina

name: Lysi
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 D2 Lysi

name: Lysimacheia
description: An ancient city in Aetolia, located 3km south of the modern city that now bears its name.

name: Macchia delle Valli, Vetralla
description: A rock-cut Etrusco-Roman sanctuary sacred to Demeter that was discovered during rescue excavations in 2006 following looting activity. The sanctuary, set in a grove, remained in use until the second century A.D.

name: Madhukeshwara temple
description: A Shiva temple at Banavasi in India whose foundation may date as early as the 7th century CE.

name: Madonna del Parto
description: Madonna del Parto is a Christian church at Sutri, dating to the thirteenth and fourteenth centuries. The rock-cut sanctuary repurposes a Mithraeum dating to the first and second centuries.

name: Madonnna del Canneto
description: The sanctuary site of Madonnna del Canneto is located at Settefrati within the confines of the Abruzzo national park in Italy. It has a long history as a sacred site, beginning with sacred activities dedicated to Mefitis in the later first millennium BCE.

name: Magnetum?
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 24 C3 Magnetum?

name: Magoula
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 54 C4 Magoula

name: Magoula Kastri
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 C1 Magoula Kastri

name: Mahabodhi Temple
description: One of the four main Buddhist pilgrimage sites, identified as the place where Gautama Buddha attained Enlightenment. The first temple on the site was built by Emperor Asoka in the third century B.C., and the present temple dates from the fifth or sixth centuries. It is one of the earliest Buddhist temples built entirely in brick, still standing in India, from the late Gupta period. The site was largely abandoned from the twelfth-eighteenth centuries. It was named a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2002.

name: Maimeh
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 92 B3 Maimeh

name: Makaravank'
description: Medieval Armenian church and monastic complex dated to the 9th-13th centuries.

name: Malchittu nuragic temple
description: A sacred structure with an 'in antis' plan located near Arzachena in Sardinia.

name: Malla
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 H4 Malla

name: Mănăstirea Halmyris
description: An Early Christian basilica and martyrium at Halmyris.

name: Mandra (Despotiko)
description: A substantial multiperiod sanctuary and associated complex on the Aegean island of Despotiko. Structures and finds date from the early Iron Age through Roman times, with some evidence for use or reuse into the Byzantine period.

name: Mandra di Mezzo
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 47 C3 Mandra di Mezzo

name: Manglisi
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 88 C3 Manglisi

name: Manna
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 C3 Manna

name: Maqam er-Rab
description: A peripteral temple built of basalt that includes an altar to Nemesis. Epigraphic data provides a chronology of late second to third centuries A.D.

name: Maqsudabad
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 94 C4 Maqsudabad

name: Mar Abay
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 89 C3 Mar Abay

name: Mar Abrohom
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 89 D3 Mar Abrohom

name: Mar Awgin
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 89 D3 Mar Awgin

name: Mar Behnam
description: The Mar Behnam Monastery may have been established as early as the fourth century CE. It was a Syriac Catholic monastery located in the village Khidr Ily in northern Iraq.

name: Mar Elian
description: The beginnings of the early Christian monastery of Saint Elian at Al-Qaryatayn, Syria, are linked with the saint Julian of Emesa. The Medieval iteration of the monastery was badly damaged - or perhaps nearly destroyed - by Islamic State terrorists in 2015.

name: Mar Matta
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 89 F4 Mar Matta

name: Mar Melki
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 89 D3 Mar Melki

name: Mar Samuel, Simeon
description: The oldest surviving Syriac Orthodox monastery in the world founded in A.D. 397 by Mor Shmu'el (Samuel) and his student Mor Shem'un (Simon) on the ruins of an ancient Assyrian temple.

name: Mar Yaqub
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 67 H2 Mar Yaqub

name: Mar Yohannon
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 89 D3 Mar Yohannon

name: Marathos
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 68 A4 Marathos

name: Marchito
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 47 E4 Marchito

name: Marcianum
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 44 F4 Marcianum

name: Marcianus Fundus
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 44 G4 Marcianus Fundus

name: Margerides
description: Remains of a large religious complex, consisting of a fanum, two small subsidiary structures, and three altars.

name: Marialba Paleochristian Basilica
description: Marialba Paleochristian Basilica dates to the fourth and fifth centuries.

name: Marica
description: Marica, an Italic goddess, was worshipped in a sacred grove located between Minturnae and the estuary of the Liris river.

name: Marisa
description: A Judean, Edomite, and later Hellenistic settlement. Destroyed ca. 40 BCE by the Parthians.

name: Markesi
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 G3 Markesi

name: Marmara sanctuary
description: An ancient sanctuary, perhaps sacred to Demeter, that was discovered during tunneling for the Corinth to Patras highway. The terraced site is located at Marmara, SSE of Aigeira. It dates from the seventh century BCE to the third century BCE.

name: Marmoutier Abbey
description: The Marmoutier Abbey located near Tours, Indre-et-Loire, France is also known as the Abbey of Marmoutier or Marmoutiers. Saint Martin of Tours (316-397) founded the abbey in 372 after he became Bishop of Tours in 371.

name: Martyrios
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 70 G2 Martyrios

name: Masamas
description: The Gallo-Roman sanctuary with twin temples was discovered in 1964 at the locality called Masamas in the commune of Saint-Léomer.

name: Mashnaqa
description: Mashnaqa, Lebanon, is the site of a monumental altar that dates to the first and second centuries A.D.

name: Matzanni
description: A sanctuary that includes a Punic temple built in white limestone, a nuraghe, and a sacred well.

name: Mausoleum and Sacred area of Hecatomnus
description: A mausoleum and sacred precinct associated with Hecatomnus of Mylasa, who was the satrap of Caria under Artaxerxes II (404–358 BC).

name: Mavrovouni
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 E4 Mavrovouni

name: Mavrovounoi
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 C3 Mavrovounoi

name: Mdoukha
description: Roman sanctuary with two small temples.

name: Mechel
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 19 D3 Mechel

name: Mefitis
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 45 B3 Mefitis

name: Megaloi Theoi (sanctuary, Imbros)
description: A sanctuary of the "Great Gods" on the Aegean island of Imbros/Gökçeada. The remains of the sanctuary are located southwest of modern Kaleköy, to the west of the airport in the vicinity of a ruined monastery of Agios Konstantinos at a place formerly known as Roxado.

name: Megiste Laura Saba
description: An Eastern Orthodox Christian monastery overlooking the Kidron Valley located between the Old City of Jerusalem and the Dead Sea.

name: Melpeia
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 58 B3 Melpeia

name: Men Askaenos, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 62 F5 Men Askaenos, T.

name: Men Karou, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 65 A2 Men Karou, T.

name: Menelaion
description: An ancient Greek sanctuary of Helen and Menelaos near ancient Therapnai in Greece.

name: Menestheus, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 26 D5 Menestheus, T.

name: Merjiyat
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 69 B2 Merjiyat

name: Meroe
description: Meroë is an ancient city located on the east bank of the Nile river near Shendi, Sudan. The city was a center of the kingdom of Kush.

name: Meroth
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 69 C3 Meroth

name: Mes Aynak
description: The site of Mes Aynak in modern Afghanistan flourished between the between the fifth and seventh centuries A.D. More than 400 Buddha statues, stupas, and a 40 ha monastery complex have been documented at the site.

name: Messon
description: An extensive ancient sanctuary complex on the island of Lesbos, thought to have been dedicated to Zeus, Hera and Dionysos and dating back to at least the 7th century BCE. A Christian basilica was built on the site in the late fourth century CE.

name: Metanoia Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 53 B2 Metanoia Mon.

name: Metapa
description: According to Polybius, this ancient settlement of Aetolia was razed by Philip of Macedon. Stephanus of Byzantium places Metapa in Acarnania. Pritchett (SAGT) meticulously describes the vicinity and identifies the site of Metapa based on ancient stone blocks in conditions of reuse and abandonment, as well as plentiful ceramic sherds in the vicinity of an older and modern church of the Analipsis on the lake shore just east of the modern village of Daphnias.

name: Meter apo Speleou, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 62 E2 Meter apo Speleou, T.

name: Meter Phileis, T.

name: Meter Theon, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 E4 Meter Theon, T.

name: Metokhi
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 C5 Metokhi

name: Metropolis (sanctuary)
description: A classical sanctuary, located somewhere near Olpai.

name: Metsopay-Vank
description: Armenian monastery and find place for a Urartian stele.

name: Mezgura
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 35 D3 Mezgura

name: Mézin
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 F1 Mézin

name: Minatiacum
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 11 E3 Minatiacum

name: Minerva Cabardiacensis
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 39 F4 Minerva Cabardiacensis

name: Minerva temple at Punta della Campanella
description: An ancient temple of Minerva, located on the ancient cape that also was known by the goddess' name.

name: Minor Acropolis, Norba
description: The so-called Minor Acropolis is located on the hill to the south of the Porta Maggiore. This area is surrounded by the urban enclosure wall and is terraced in polygonal masonry. Two platform temples of unknown assignation are to be found here.

name: Mithraeum at Fertőrákos
description: A well-preserved Mithraeum with modern stone vault above ground and site below in Fertőrákos, Hungary, at the site of ancient Scarbantia.

name: Mithraeum at Mundelsheim
description: A second-century Mithraeum is located in the area of the municipality of Mundelsheim in the Ludwigsburg district of Baden-Württemberg, Germany.

name: Mithraeum at Segontium
description: A mithraeum was excavated at Segontium (modern Caernarfon) in 1959, ca. 137m from the east corner of the Roman auxiliary fort. The site was abandoned in the late fourth century A.D.

name: Mithraeum at Vulci
description: A mithraeum was discovered at Vulci in 1975 during excavations of a Roman aristocratic house. The shrine was well preserved with two tauroctony scenes dating to the third century. The site was destroyed in the fourth century A.D.

name: Mithraeum III at Poetovio
description: The third of five shrines sacred to Mithras found near the site of Poetovio.

name: Mogao Caves
description: Buddhist cave monastery complex in the cliffs above the Dachuan River near the Dunhaung Oasis, constructed in 366 CE and in use until the 14th Century CE. The caves contain thousands of murals and painted sculptures, and thousands of manuscripts in Chinese, Tibetan, Uyghur, Sogdian, Sanskrit, and Khotanese were stored in the so-called "Library Cave." The Mogao Caves were added to UNESCO's World Heritage List in 1987.

name: Möhn
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 11 G3 Möhn

name: Mollarella
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 47 D4 Mollarella

name: Mollis
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 19 B2 Mollis

name: Monasteraki
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 C3 Monasteraki

name: Monastero di San Mamiliano
description: The monastery of San Mamiliano located on the island of Montecristo.

name: Monastery of S. Elia Vecchio
description: A ruined ninth century Christian monastery in Calabria.

name: Monastery of San Juan de Caaveiro
description: A Christian monastery established in AD 934.

name: Monastery of Simeon Stylites the Younger
description: A sixth-century AD monastery named for Simeon Stylites the Younger who lived atop a pillar on Thaumaston Oros.

name: Mons Seleucus
description: Mons Seleucus was the site of the famous battle between Constantius II and the usurper Magnentius in AD 353. The Gallo-Roman vicus at La Bâtie-Montsaléon included a Gallo-Roman sanctuary.

name: Mont-Rivel
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 18 C3 Mont-Rivel

name: Montbouy
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 H1 Montbouy

name: Monte d'Accoddi
description: A ramped, megalithic structure in Sardinia dated to around 2700-2000 BC.

name: Monte del Fico
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 44 E2 Monte del Fico

name: Monte Li Santi
description: A Faliscan sanctuary located on the slopes of Monte Li Santi.

name: Monte Monaco di Gioia
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 44 F3 Monte Monaco di Gioia

name: Monte Papalucio
description: The sanctuary of Monte Papalucio was an important Messapian sanctuary. It is located on the mountain of the same name in the territory of Oria in the province of Brindisi. The deities Demeter and Persephone were venerated in this cave sanctuary.

name: Monte S. Martino
description: The remains of a multi-period pre-Roman and Roman sanctuary.

name: Monte Torre Maggiore
description: An Italic temple complex in Umbria of the first millennium B.C. that includes the so-called "Ara maior".

name: Monte Tosto
description: An Etruscan and Roman site lying 4 km to the west of Caere along the monumental road leading to Pyrgi. Until the early 1960s, the site was known for the monumental tumulus that Mengarelli had excavated in the 1920s. Subsequent excavation revealed other evidence, including an elite compound that served as a sanctuary.

name: Montebuono
description: The remains of a villa were excavated near the church of San Pietro ad centum muros in the nineteenth century. Based on epigraphic evidence, the site is connected with M. Vipsanius Agrippa.

name: Monteverde
description: The eleventh century monastery of Santa Maria di Monteverde sits atop and reuses elements of Roman and pre-Roman settlements on the hilltop.

name: Montreuil-Bellay
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 E1 Montreuil-Bellay

name: Morulls
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 F4 Morulls

name: Msellata
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 35 F2 Msellata

name: Mselliten
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 35 G3 Mselliten

name: Muk
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 94 C4 Muk

name: Mura Abbandonate, Sanctuary of Fondo Decina
description: A terraced sanctuary of the Samnite culture that was in use from the archaic period to the third century B.C.

name: Musaifna
description: Excavations at Tell Museifneh (Eski Mosul) in the 1980s revealed remains of a well-built stone building that was identified as a Christian basilica dating to the seventh century A.D. The apisdal basilica measured ca. 50 x 50 meters.

name: Musawwarat
description: A Meroitic temple complex in modern Sudan, located 180 km northeast of Khartoum, 20 km north of Naqa and approximately 25 km south-east of the Nile. The complex dates to the early Meroitic period (third century B.C.). In 2011 the site was enrolled as a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

name: Mushabbak Basilica
description: The remains of a well-preserved Byzantine church (ca. 470) located five km west of the Monastery of St Simeon. The ruins lie near the town of Daret A'zzeh.

name: Mut el-Kharab
description: The settlement of Mut el-Kharab, located in Egypt's Dakhleh Oasis. A temple to the god Seth was built at this location.

name: Mygdaleza basilica
description: The remains of an Early Christian basilica dating to the fifth and sixth centuries.

name: Myrtou
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 C2 Myrtou

name: Mysia (settlement)
description: An ancient settlement in the Argolid, mentioned by Pausanias, who highlights there a sanctuary of Demeter. This settlement is probably to be identified with the modern Phyktia.

name: Nagos
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 C4 Nagos

name: Naisar
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 92 G3 Naisar

name: Nālandā Mahavihara
description: Buddhist monastery and educational center founded during the Gupta Empire (5th century CE). The site was destroyed around 1200 CE. It was located in ancient and medieval Magadha (modern-day Bihar), eastern India.

name: Nanteuil-sur-Aisne
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 11 E3 Nanteuil-sur-Aisne

name: Naqsh-i Bahram
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 94 B3 Naqsh-i Bahram

name: Naqsh-i Rajab
description: An archaeological site with rock-cut reliefs made under the Sasanian kings Ardašir I (224-241) and Shapur I (241-272).

name: Narbek
description: A rock-cut church located about 5km west of the modern town of Omar Mukhtar in Libya.

name: Narmouthis
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 75 D2 Narmouthis

name: Natanz
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 92 G3 Natanz

name: Naudaran
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 94 C5 Naudaran

name: Navan
description: A ceremonial site of pre-Christian Gaelic Ireland and the capital of the Ulaid. The site, located near Armagh, Northern Ireland, consists of a fortified settlement with a ceremonial building.

name: Naveta of Torre Llisà
description: A naviform temple located on Minorca.

name: Navish
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 92 F2 Navish

name: Nea Moni
description: An eleventh-century monastery on Chios built by Constantine IX Monomachos. The site was listed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1990.

name: Nebi Safa
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 69 C2 Nebi Safa

name: Nebo Mons (church)
description: Site of a modern church and monastery atop Mount Nebo in modern Jordan on a site commemorated by earlier churches built on a site traditionally held to be the burial location of the prophet Moses. The first ancient church here is mentioned earliest in the itinerary of Egeria.

name: Neklone
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 75 D2 Neklone

name: Nekromanteion
description: An ancient sanctuary of necromancy sacred to Hades and Persephone located on the banks of the Acheron River in Epirus, near the ancient city of Ephyra.

name: Nemora Vacunae
description: Shrine dedicated to a rural divinity of the Sabina.

name: Nemrut Dağ
description: A major sanctuary of Coammagene and site of the mausoleum of Antiochus I (69–34 B.C.). A UNESCO World Heritage Site located on modern Mount Nemrut in Turkey's Adıyaman province.

name: Nemus Sorabense/Sorabile
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 48 B2 Nemus Sorabense/Sorabile

name: Nessana
description: Nessana is an ancient site located in the Negev Desert. Established during the Hellenistic period, Nessana has numerous occupational phases including as the site of a Roman military garrison in the fourth century CE. The site flourishes in connection with the Christian pilgrimage route linking the Holy Land to Mount Sinai (sixth and seventh centuries CE).

name: Nettleton
description: A significant cult center at Nettleton Shrub in Wiltshire was dedicated to Apollo Cunomaglus.

name: Neuvy-sur-Barangeon
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 H1 Neuvy-sur-Barangeon

name: New Year's Festival House at Assur
description: As part of his post-689-BC religious reforms, the Neo-Assyrian king Sennacherib (704–681 BC) had a new New Year’s festival house (akītu-house) constructed outside the western wall of Assur. This was an attempt to replicate the cultic topography of Babylon. The Sumerian ceremonial names of that holy building and its cella are Eabbaugga ("House Where Tiāmat Is Put To Death") and Edubdubabba ("House That Makes Tiāmat Tremble”) respectively.

name: Neyrac-les-Bains
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 17 C4 Neyrac-les-Bains

name: Nike (Monument at Marathon)
description: A monument to the Battle of Marathon. Eugene Vanderpool describes the location of the monument as lying in the northeastern part of the plain of Marathon. A Christian chapel known as the Panagia Mesosporitissa marks the vicinity of this monument. In the area of the chapel are a well that reuses ancient marble and the ruins of a medieval tower that was built from re-used ancient blocks, including a massive Ionic column capital and two large column drums.

name: Nistos
description: A sanctuary on Mont Sacon at an elevation of 1541 meters above sea level.

name: Northern sanctuary at Kythnos
description: Religious structure in Kythnos constructed in the Archaic period.

name: Nostra Segnora de Mesumundu
description: Nostra Segnora de Mesumundu ("Our Lady of Mesumundu") is located in the territory of Siligo, Sardinia, Italy. This archaeological site preserves remains of multiple architectural phases. A mid-Imperial (likely second-century CE) Roman complex, which included baths, was reused, in part, for the construction of a Christian church in the sixth century.

name: Notre-Dame-de-Laval
description: A pre-Roman and Gallo-Roman sanctuary at the locality called l'Ermitage de Notre-Dame-de-Laval.

name: Nubian Lion Temple at Naqa
description: The Temple of Apedemak, also known as the Lion Temple, is located to the west of the temple of Amun.

name: Nymphaion
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 C2 Nymphaion

name: Nymphaion/Balaneion Tiberinon
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 67 B4 Nymphaion/Balaneion Tiberinon

name: Oberdorla
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 12 D1 Oberdorla

name: Oberlienz
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 19 E3 Oberlienz

name: Octon
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 15 A2 Octon

name: Odry
description: The modern Polish city of Odry is the site of a major "stone circles" cemetery of the iron age Wielbark Culture.

name: Oisseau-Le-Petit
description: Oisseau-Le-Petit is the site of a Gallo-Roman fanum.

name: Olympieion
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 47 G4 Olympieion

name: Onia
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 G1 Onia

name: Optatianai, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 52 F3 Optatianai, Mon.

name: Oracular sanctuary at Oinoe
description: Located to the east of the medieval tower at Oinoe are the remains of a rectangular peristylar building measuring approximately 13.60 x 17.30 meters. The peristyle used marble columns set atop marble pilasters. Spyridon Marinatos discovered the site in 1972.

name: Orbaniacum
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 G1 Orbaniacum

name: Örendibi
description: Modern name for ruins of a Byzantine church and surrounding rural settlement in eastern Rough Cilicia

name: Orsingen
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 19 A2 Orsingen

name: Oscata di Sopra
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 45 B2 Oscata di Sopra

name: Oulippada?
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 5 inset Oulippada?

name: Pachyammos sanctuary
description: The remains of a robbed-out Hellenistic temple complex were excavated at Pachyammos to the south of Paphos, Cyprus, between 2015 and 2019. The site included a peripteral temple in antis.

name: Pagai
description: A fortified port located on the Gulf of Corinth near Alepokhori.

name: Pagania Sanctuary (Aigies, Laconia)
description: A rural sanctuary, probably of Artemis, located about a kilometer north-northwest of modern Aigies in Laconia. It is surely to be associated with ancient Aigiai, which was located less than two kilometers to the southwest.

name: Pagans Hill Roman Temple
description: A Romano-British temple excavated on Pagans Hill at Chew Stoke, Somerset.

name: Pagus Assalitanus
description: Pagus Assalitanus, located in Africa Proconsularis, lies some 6 km southeast of Thubursicu Bure.

name: Palaio Pretorio
description: The ruins of a colonnaded structure in marble, perhaps a temple; a well / cistern is also observed by Sealy.

name: Palaiomonastero
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 D2 Palaiomonastero

name: Paleochristian basilica
description: A Paleochristian basilica was discovered at the eighth mile of the Via Tiburtina in 2003. The subterranean basilica reused an earlier quarry and may be related to San Sinforosa. The site dates from the late fourth to the middle of the fifth century CE.

name: paleochristian basilica of Lugdunum Convenarum
description: The paleochristian basilica of Saint-Bertrand-de-Comminges.

name: Paleochristian basilica, Illa del Rei
description: The Paleochristian basilica located on Illa del Rei dates to the sixth century.

name: Paleochristian Church, Lin
description: A paleochristian church located in Lin, Korçë County, Albania, with fourth- and fifth-century mosaics.

name: Palike
description: An ancient settlement in east central Sicily, atop the Rocchicella, which included a sanctuary to the Divine Palikoi (a pair of boiling lakes) within a grotto found on the hillside.

name: Pampligua
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 24 H2 Pampligua

name: Pan, Cave of
description: The Phyle Cave located on Mount Parnes near Fyli (Phyle), Greece, is a small cave that served in antiquity as a shrine sacred to the god Pan. It is one of five such cave sanctuaries dedicated to Pan known in ancient Attica.

name: Panagia Angeloktistos
description: The church of Panagia tis Angeloktistis or Panagia Angeloktisti is a Byzantine church located in the village of Kiti, Cyprus. Its origins lies in the fifth and sixth centuries. It was listed as a tentative UNESCO World Heritage site in 2015.

name: Panagia Arkoudiotissa
description: The Arkoudiotissa Cave on Crete has been the site of ritual activity since antiquity. The cave's cultic association stems from a rock formation that looks like a bear (Arkoudiotissa ("she-bear")).

name: Panagia Kanakaria
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 E2 Panagia Kanakaria

name: Panagia tis Kyras
description: A twelfth-century CE Greek Orthodox church located north of modern Livadia on Cyprus' Karpasia peninsula. Its apse had been adapted from part of an older building and contained a 7th-century mosaic of the Theotokos (i.e., Mary, mother of Jesus).

name: Panetelle
description: A sanctuary with votive deposits dating to the Hellenistic period at Panetelle di Mondragone.

name: Panionion
description: Located near Mt. Mycale, the Panionion was an Ionian sanctuary dedicated to Poseidon Helikonios that served as the meeting place of the Ionian League.

name: Panóias
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 24 D3 Panóias

name: Pānṛ I
description: Buddhist temple complex in Swat, Pakistan occupied from the 1st century CE until the end of the 4th-5th centuries CE.

name: Paphor, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 E2 Paphor, Mon.

name: Paptoulis?
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 81 C2 Paptoulis?

name: Parco archeologico Griso-Laboccetta
description: An Archaic to Hellenistic sanctuary sacred to Demeter.

name: Parembole?
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 81 C2 Parembole?

name: Parthenion, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 23 G4 Parthenion, T.

name: Passy
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 17 H2 Passy

name: Patriacum
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 H2 Patriacum

name: Patriasdorf
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 19 E3 Patriasdorf

name: Pattadakal
description: A group of temples featuring nine Hindu temples and a Jain sanctuary dating to the seventh and eighth centuries CE and connected with the Chalukya dynasty.

name: Pecë
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 54 B2 Pecë

name: Pelm
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 11 G2 Pelm

name: Per-Ptah
description: The site of Per-Ptah, meaning "House of Ptah", lies some 90 km south of Aswan and received a temple dedicated to pharaoh Ramesses II. Some of the architectural and sculptural remains of the temple were dismantled and relocated to the site of New Kalabsha in the 1960s during the construction of the Aswan High Dam.

name: Pera Milous
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 58 G2 Pera Milous

name: Pergamos
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 D2 Pergamos

name: Peroushtitsa
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 22 B6 Peroushtitsa

description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 42 F4 Pescara

name: Petit-Mars
