name: Petrera
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 D2 Petrera

name: Petrera
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 C3 Petrera

name: Pfaffenhofen
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 19 D2 Pfaffenhofen

name: Phanai
description: A large ancient sanctuary of Apollo, located on the southwest coast of the island of Chios.

name: Phasiane Theos
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 87 G2 Phasiane Theos

name: Phlabianai
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 64 B3 Phlabianai

name: Phosphoros/Lux Dubia (sanctuary)
description: Strabo describes this sanctuary in his narrative of sailing up the Guadalquivir River. His text mentions a sanctuary of Phosphorus - this is likely Diana - that is known as "Lux Dubia" (Strabo transliterates "Lucem dubiam" to Greek characters, thus λοῦκεμ δουβίαν).

name: Piana del Lago Sanctuary
description: An Etruscan sanctuary that was founded at the end of the 5th century BCE and saw activity as late as the reign of Nero. Evidence has also been found for activity in the area during the Neolithic and the Bronze Age. The site is located on the southern shore of the Lac de Bolsena in the modern Italian municipality of Montefiascone.

name: Piano dei Tivoli
description: Archaeological excavations conducted at the locality known as Piano dei Tivoli revealed, by means of numerous fragments of architectural terracottas, a sanctuary of the Hellenistic period. There is also evidence of settlement during the fifth and fourth centuries BCE.

name: Pietra Rossa
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 47 E4 Pietra Rossa

name: Pieve di San Giovanni Battista a Sillano
description: A ruined church that was once part of the diocese of Volterra, San Giovanni Battista a Sillano is unique in Tuscany for having a Romanesque-Norman façade. The ruined edifice likely dates to the twelfth and thirteenth centuries although the parish exists as early as the mid-tenth century.

name: Pieve di San Pietro ad Mensulas
description: A Romanesque church located in the Italian comune of Sinalunga (Siena). Saint Donatus of Arezzo may have founded the church in the fourth century.

name: Pieve Socana
description: Pieve di Sant'Antonino a Socana, a Medieval parish church, is built atop the remains of an Etruscan sanctuary.

name: Pigi
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 60 D2 Pigi

name: Pillerhöhe
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 19 C2 Pillerhöhe

name: Pirdop
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 22 B6 Pirdop

name: Pizzica
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 45 E4 Pizzica

name: Plataea
description: Plataea is an ancient city of Boeotia, located south of Thebes. In 479 BC Plataea was the site of the Battle of Plataea, in which an allied Greek army defeated the Persians. Plataea was destroyed in the Peloponnesian War by Thebes and Sparta in 427 BC and rebuilt in 386 BC.

name: Plöckenpass
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 19 E3 Plöckenpass

name: Ploutonion at Hierapolis
description: The Ploutonion at Hierapolis is a walled sanctuary dedicated to Pluto and Kore.

name: Poggio Colla
description: An Etruscan settlement located in the Mugello valley.

name: Pommern-Martberg
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 11 H2 Pommern-Martberg

name: Pompeiacum/Vernemetis
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 F4 Pompeiacum/Vernemetis

name: Porto Cesareo
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 45 G4 Porto Cesareo

name: Portonaccio sanctuary at Veii
description: The extraurban sanctuary of Minerva at Portonaccio is an important Etruscan cult center. The temple dates ca. 510 B.C. and was adorned with acroterial sculptures, notably the famous "Apollo of Veii".

name: Poseidon Phytalmios?, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 A4 Poseidon Phytalmios?, T.

name: Poseidon Porthmios, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 60 E4 Poseidon Porthmios, T.

name: Poseidon, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 86 C2 Poseidon, T.

name: Poseidon?, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 A3 Poseidon?, T.

name: Poseidon?, T.
description: Poseidon?, T. a sanctuary of Poseidon on the island of Samos.

name: Potamos, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 62 F2 Potamos, T.

name: Pouzauges
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 E2 Pouzauges

name: Pranu Muttedu
description: Pranu Muttedu is a pre- and protohistoric archaeological site located near Goni, Sardinia. The complex includes standing stones (menhirs) and prehistoric built tombs (domus de janas). The site dates to the Late Neolithic (3200 – 2800 BC) and Early Chalcolithic (2800 – 2600 BC) periods.

name: Prdejci
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 50 B2 Prdejci

name: Preah Ko
description: Temple constructed in the city of Hariharalaya, dedicated in 880 CE by the Khmer king Indravarman I.

name: Prepelaion?
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 E5 Prepelaion?

name: Prinias (Patela)
description: An Iron Age settlement on the Patela plateau north of the modern village of Prinias; its ancient name is uncertain. The site is notable for its occupation from the end of the Bronze Age through to the Archaic period, as well as for the monumental architecture and Orientalizing sculpture of its Buildings ('Temples') A and B.

name: Profitis Elias
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 54 C4 Profitis Elias

name: Prummu?/Primii?
description: The one-time capital of the ancient Kerma culture in Nubia.

name: Psaropyrgos
description: An ancient temple of Poseidon, known today as the "Tomb of Homer", located on a height at the northern end of the Aegean island of Ios. Some funerary epigraphy has also been noted at the site.

name: Ptghnavank
description: Church building in the modern village of Ptghni, Kotayk province, Armenia, dated to the 6th-7th centuries CE.

name: Ptoou Nkalamon
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 75 D3 Ptoou Nkalamon

name: Punic-Roman Sanctuary, Kamart
description: A Punic-Roman sanctuary, possibly of Hygieia/Salus or Aesculapius/Eshmun was identified to the northwest of Carthage in the direction of Cape Kamart (Tunisia), mostly on the basis of an assemblage of extremely fragmentary polychromatic terracotta statues, numbering at least 30.

name: Punta della Vipera Etruscan temple
description: A coastal Etruscan sanctuary that dates ca. 540-530 B.C. with additional architectural phases dating to the fourth through second centuries B.C.

name: Pusht-i Arisht
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 92 B3 Pusht-i Arisht

name: Puy-Lautard
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 G3 Puy-Lautard

name: Pyra
description: Pyra is a place with preserved ruins of a Doric temple dating to the third century B.C. The sources connect Pyra with the pyre of the hero Herakles.

name: Pyramid-complex of Senusret III
description: Funerary complex of King Senusret III at Dahshur including a pyramid.

name: Pyrgi
description: Pyrgi is an ancient Etruscan port city on the Tyrrhenian coast of Italy. A Roman colony was established at the site by 191 B.C. In 1964, the bilingual 'Pyrgi Plaques' were discovered during the course of excavations.

name: Qadamgah
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 94 D4 Qadamgah

name: Qadboun
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 68 B3 Qadboun

name: Qafr Zebad
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 69 C2 Qafr Zebad

name: Qalaa
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 68 B5 Qalaa

name: Qalaat Faqra
description: A Roman-era sanctuary complex in Lebanon with multiple altars and two temples. On the basis of epigraphic evidence, one of these temples seems to have been dedicated to Atargatis and the other possibly to Zeus Beelgalassos. There is some evidence for worship also of Zeus Heliopolis and for the Roman imperial cult. A Byzantine basilica was later built on the site.

name: Qalat el-Baben
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 80 B4 Qalat el-Baben

name: Qalb Lozé
description: An ancient place, located in modern Syria, with remains of an ancient basilica. Inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2011 as part of the "Ancient Villages of Northern Syria."

name: Qalbloze
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 67 D4 Qalbloze

name: Qanat-i Bagh
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 94 B4 Qanat-i Bagh

name: Qasr el-Aguz
description: Site in Luxor (Egypt) associated with a Ptolemaic temple dedicated to the god Thoth.

name: Qasr el-Banat
description: The remains of an ancient temple at Qasr el Banat, Lebanon,

name: Qasr el-Sagha temple
description: Temple located north of Lake Qarun in the Fayyum Depression. Tentatively dated to the Old Kingdom period, the temple may have been dedicated to the god Sobek.

name: Qasr Libya, West Church
description: At Qasr Libya, the ancient Theodorias, two churhes have been identified. The Byzantine settlement was refounded under Justinian in 539 CE.

name: Qasr Neba
description: Qasr Neba is the site of a Roman temple in the Bekaa valley of Lebanon.

name: Qasr Nemrud
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 69 D2 Qasr Nemrud

name: Qasr Serij
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 89 E4 Qasr Serij

name: Qenneshre
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 67 G3 Qenneshre

name: Quarrush
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 37 B1 Quarrush

name: Raiano
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 42 F4 Raiano

name: Ramappa Temple
description: A thirteenth-century temple that was built during the period of the Kakatiya ruler Ganapati Deva.

name: Rantidi
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 B3 Rantidi

name: Ras Abu el Daraq
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 76 B3 Ras Abu el Daraq

name: Ras el-Haddagia
description: A sanctuary of Ammon was located at Ras el-Haddagia. The rectangular temple (7.05 x 2.95 m) included an altar and a portico.

name: Ras il-Wardija
description: A Punic-Roman sanctuary site located on the western coast of Gozo (Gaulos), Malta.

name: Re-Buried Hellenistic Temple and Workshops
description: Excavations on site of post office at 49 Ben Ami St. revealed Hellenistic remains, including a dedication to Zeus Soter on behalf of Antiochos VII by a courtier (SEG XIX 9094). This inscription was associated with Hellenistic architecture of a temple-like character. Nearby workshops also uncovered.

name: Reccopolis
description: Reccopolis is an urban, Visigothic, ex-novo foundation made under king Liuvigild in A.D. 578. The archaeological remains at the site include a palace complex, public area, basilica, walls, and aqueduct. The site is known today by the Spanish version of its Latin name (Recópolis); it is located near the modern village of Zorita de los Canes in the Spanish province of Guadelajara.

name: Red Hill Roman Temple
description: Site of a Roman temple and possible small town near the confluence of the rivers Soar and Trent near Ratclifffe-on-Soar, Nottinghamshire. Findspot of at least three curse tablets (defixiones).

name: Rephidim
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 76 D4 Rephidim

name: Resras
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 80 B4 Resras

name: Ribemont-sur-Ancre
description: Ribemont-sur-Ancre is the site of a Celtic and Gallo-Roman sanctuary located on the right bank of the Ancre river, a tributary of the Somme. Roger Agache discovered the site in 1962. The site is vast in its area, covering some 80 ha.

name: Ried
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 12 H4 Ried

name: Rijelat Umm Kubar
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 91 C4 Rijelat Umm Kubar

name: Rijsbergen
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 11 E1 Rijsbergen

name: Risqah
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 76 G3 Risqah

name: Riva S. Vitale
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 39 D2 Riva S. Vitale

name: Robigo?, T.
description: The sanctuary of the god Robigus, located at the sixth mile of the Via Cassia. The Romans celebrated the festival of Robigalia on 25 April.

name: Roccadaspide
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 45 B4 Roccadaspide

name: Roccaspromonte
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 44 G2 Roccaspromonte

name: Rock-cut Sanctuary of Letoids and Nymphs
description: The Elmalı Museum's 2011 discovery of a rock-cut relief and many broken stelai near the fountain known as Sarı Mehmet Çeşmesi, in the Yukarıovacık Yayla (plateau), on the western slopes of Temrencik Tepe, on the border between the territory of ancient Choma and Oinoanda, points to the existence of a spring sanctuary of Leto, her children Apollo and Artemis (the Letoids), and perhaps also 9 nymphs.

name: Roda
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 54 A2 Roda

name: Rogeban
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 35 E3 Rogeban

name: Roma et Augustus, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 65 D2 Roma et Augustus, T.

name: Roman spring sanctuary near Heckenmünster
description: An extensive complex of structures dating to the 1st - 3rd centuries of the Roman imperial period sited at a pair of springs known today as "Viktoriaquelle" and "Schwefelquelle," near modern Heckenmünster in German. This site, whose ancient name and tutelary deities are not known, was extensively excavated in the 1960s. It comprised religious and performative spaces, as well as baths and hostelry.

name: Roman Temple at Maarab
description: Site of a Roman temple overlooking Jounieh Bay

name: Roman temple of Córdoba
description: A pseudodipteral, hexastyle, Corinthian order temple begun under Claudius and completed under Domitian. The temple is likely sacred to the imperial cult.

name: Roman temple of Vic
description: The Roman temple of Vic dates to the second century A.D.

name: Roman temple, Capo Mulini
description: The remains of an early Imperial Roman temple on Sicily's Cape Mulini.

name: Roman temples at Chieti
description: Structures thought to be temples set within a sacred area that includes a sacred well.

name: Roquepertuse
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 15 E2 Roquepertuse

name: Rossano
description: Rossano di Vaglio, a fourth century BC site dedicated to Mefitis, was a Lucanian federal sanctuary.

name: Roujan
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 15 A2 Roujan

name: Rouvroy-les-Merles
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 11 C3 Rouvroy-les-Merles

name: Rouweyha
description: An ancient place, located in modern Syria, with remains of an ancient basilica, residential buildings, and several tombs. Inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2011 as part of the "Ancient Villages of Northern Syria."

name: Ruoti
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 45 C3 Ruoti

name: Ruus el-Aslab
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 38 D1 Ruus el-Aslab

name: Ruwwafah
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 83 B4 Ruwwafah

name: S. Alessandro
description: The basilica and catacombs of S. Alessandro are located at the seventh milestone of the Via Nomentana.

name: S. Anatolia
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 42 E4 S. Anatolia

name: S. Autonomos, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 52 F3 S. Autonomos, Mon.

name: S. Buono
description: A fortified town near Chieti in the Abruzzo, the ancient phases of S. Buono include evidence for an indigenous sacred site of the later first millennium BC. Among the finds are votive objects dedicated to Herakles.

name: S. Clemens
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 E2 S. Clemens

name: S. Cornelia, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 44 B1 S. Cornelia, Mon.

name: S. Cristina d’Aro
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 I4 S. Cristina d’Aro

name: S. Cugat
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 H4 S. Cugat

name: S. Desideratus, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 18 B3 S. Desideratus, Mon.

name: S. Eulalia de Bóveda
description: Located some 16 km from Lugo, S. Eulalia de Bóveda is the location of a vaulted, three-aisled, underground building, likely dating to the middle Imperial period and functioning as a nymphaeum.

name: S. Giovanni in Galdo
description: Site of a small Samnitic sanctuary atop Colle Rimontato (709 m. a.s.l.)

name: S. Giovanni Lippioni
description: S. Giovanni Lippioni is the site of a pre-Roman sanctuary that was later repurposed as a Christian church.

name: S. Jorge
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 D3 S. Jorge

name: S. Lidano
description: The so-called "Archi di San Lidano" are the remains of a Roman stone bridge in the Pontine plain, near the modern locality of Sezze Scalo. The bridge likely dates originally to the second century B.C. Evidence of an ancient sanctuary have also been detected in the vicinity.

name: S. Maria d’Artès
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 G4 S. Maria d’Artès

name: S. Maria di Galeria
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 B1 S. Maria di Galeria

name: S. Maria di Minerbe
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 39 H2 S. Maria di Minerbe

name: S. Martinus
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 44 E3 S. Martinus

name: S. Pantaleón
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 B3 S. Pantaleón

name: S. Porphyrius
description: The oldest Orthodox church in Gaza, sacred to the fifth century Saint Porphyrius whose tomb lies within the church.

name: S. Quiricus, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 I3 S. Quiricus, Mon.

name: S. Salvatore di Cabras
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 48 A3 S. Salvatore di Cabras

name: S. Sebastianus
description: The protocoenobium of San Sebastiano is a monastery located in the territory of Alatri, to the east of the city center on the slopes of Mount Pizzuto.

name: S. Sebastianus
description: S. Sebastianus (San Sebastiano fuori le mura) is a Christian basilica outside the walls of Rome; the earliest phase belongs to the fourth century AD.

name: S. Simeon (monastery)
description: The fortified Saint Simeon Monastery (Aswan, Egypt) dates to the seventh century. It was initially sacred to the local saint Anba Hedra. The site was rebuilt in the tenth century and re-dedicated to Saint Simeon. Saladin partially destroyed the monastery in 1173.

name: S. Thekla, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 66 D4 S. Thekla, Mon.

name: S. Theodora
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 53 A2 S. Theodora

name: S. Victoriano
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 F3 S. Victoriano

name: S. Zion, Mon./Pharroa
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 65 C5 S. Zion, Mon./Pharroa

name: Sabiona
description: A seventeenth century Benedictine nunnery located on a site that shows occupational evidence stretching back to the Neolithic period and that includes substantial Roman and Late Antique phases.

name: Sacellum of the Augustales
description: The so-called "Sacellum of the Augustales" at Misenum is an architectural complex located near the theater that was first built during the Iulio-Claudian period and dedicated to the Imperial cult. The complex was renovated during the second century, at which time one Cassia Victoria re-dedicated the shrine in honor of her husband, L. Laecanius Primitivus, who was a priest of the Augustales during the reign of Marcus Aurelius.

name: Sacoué
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 F2 Sacoué

name: Sacro Speco
description: The Monastery of Saint Benedict or Monastero di San Benedetto Sacro Speco. The Shrine of the Sacred Cave of St. Benedict (Sacro Speco di San Benedetto) at Subiaco is a significant monastic shrine connected to the Roman Catholic saint Benedict. At the site is found a fresco-adorned cave linked to the saint, dating to the eighth century.

name: Sail-sur-Couzan
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 17 B2 Sail-sur-Couzan

name: Saint-Pierre-d'Aurillac
description: Remains of Gallo-Roman substructures that support the choir of the present church.

name: Saint-Plancard
description: The Chapel of Saint Jean is built atop a temple once dedicated to Iuppiter Optimus Maximus.

name: Saint-Sernin-sur-Rance
description: Remains of a rustic temple and a menhir were discovered in the early 1960s near Boutaran.

name: Salins-les-Thermes
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 17 H3 Salins-les-Thermes

name: Salt-en-Donzy
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 17 C2 Salt-en-Donzy

name: Samnite Sanctuary at Pietrabbondante
description: An ancient sanctuary on the slope of Monte Saraceno in the modern Italian comune of Pietrabbondante.

name: San Giacomo di Rialto
description: A church in Venice, Italy. San Giacomo di Rialto is claimed to be the oldest church in the city, with AD 421 as the traditional date of its consecration. The first documented attestations of the church belong to AD 1152.

name: San Giovanni de Butris
description: A church of the eleventh and twelfth century built along the line of the Roman Via Flaminia. The church incorporates Roman remains, including those of a bridge.

name: San Nicola di Trullas
description: A Medieval church on the island of Sardinia that is dated to 1133.

name: San Pietro a Badia Pozzeveri
description: An abbey with tenth century origins connected to the pilgrimage route known as the Via Francigena.

name: San Pietro ad Oratorium
description: A Romanesque-style Roman Catholic church and Benedictine monastery consecrated in 1117 but with possible origins in the eighth century.

name: San Pietro di Cantoni Italic sanctuary
description: An extra-urban Italic sanctuary located in the territory of ancient Saepinum.

name: Sanahin Monastery
description: Monastery complex in northern Armenia constructed during the 10th century CE.

name: Sanctuaries of Apollo Deiradiotes and Athena Oxyderkes
description: At Argos, Pausanias mentions both the sanctuary of Apollo Deiradiotes (Apollo of the ridge) and the sanctuary of Athena Oxyderkes (Athena the clear-sighted).

name: Sanctuary at Agios Konstantinos (Methana)
description: A Late Helladic sanctuary was excavated by Helene Konstolakis-Jiannopoulou in 1990 near the chapel of Agios Konstantinos and Elenis. Activity at this site dates between 1500 and 1300 B.C.

name: Sanctuary at Leben
description: Leben is known for a sanctuary of Asklepios dating to the first and second centuries CE.

name: Sanctuary at Lucus Feroniae
description: The sanctuary and grove of Feronia located at the site of Lucus Feroniae.

name: Sanctuary of Aphrodite Paphia
description: A major sanctuary to Aphrodite on Cyprus; activity is found as early as the third millennium BCE.

name: Sanctuary of Apollo
description: The Sanctuary of Apollo near Amyklai is a stoa-like building that enclosed a colossal statue of Apollo on three sides and was decorated with reliefs. The tomb of Hyacinthus is used as a statue base, combining the two local deities together.

name: Sanctuary of Apollo at Halieis
description: The Sanctuary of Apollo at Halieis is a temple complex dedicated primarily to Apollo, constructed outside of the city of Halieis and dating between the seventh and fourth centuries BCE.

name: Sanctuary of Artemis Anaiitis in Lydia
description: Pausanias describes the Sanctuary of Artemis Anaiitis in Lydia in his third book. Central to his discussion is the xoanon of Artemis that may have been present in the sanctuary, one which he associates with the object Orestes has carried off from Taurus, an episode described by Euripides in his play, Iphigenia in Tauris.

name: Sanctuary of Artemis at Avlonas
description: A sanctuary possibly sacred to Artemis on the island of Lemnos.

name: Sanctuary of Artemis Limnatis in the Taygetos
description: A sanctuary in the Taygetos mountains, attested in both literary and epigraphic texts, its precise location cannot yet be determined; however scattered and reused architectural components and inscriptions have been found in the area of the Chapel of Panagia Kapsocherovolousa, Volimos, northwest of modern Alagonia in the Peloponnese. Of uncertain foundation date, but confirmed by the Roman Senate under Tiberius as belonging to the Messenians (as part of the Ager Denthaliatis), rather than the Spartans.

name: Sanctuary of Artemis Limnatis on Mount Lapithos
description: This sanctuary, located near the modern village of Artemida in Elis (Greece), was excavated in the early 20th century.

name: Sanctuary of Asklepios, Balagrae
description: The Sanctuary of Asklepios at Balagrae in Cyrenaica is dated to the Hadrianic period on the basis of epigraphic evidence.

name: Sanctuary of Athena Itonia
description: A pan-thessaliote sanctuary, located at modern Philia Karditsis in Greece.

name: Sanctuary of Bendis
description: Sanctuary of Bendis located on the Acropolis of Salamis

name: Sanctuary of Dionysos
description: The Sanctuary of Dionysos, which includes a theater, choregic monument, statue bases, and at least one temple, formed part of the civic center of the Attic deme of Ikarion.

name: Sanctuary of Eleusinian Demeter in Laconia
description: An extensive sanctuary mentioned by Pausanias and located at the modern village of Kalyvia Sochas by virtue of the discovery of a number of inscriptions on the site. Very little of the sanctuary is visible today, as the site was damaged by a number of floods and only ever partly excavated. Modern signage in the village marks the location of some finds.

name: Sanctuary of Erynike Aphrodite
description: A sanctuary of Aphrodite Erykine near Psophis in Achaia that is mentioned by Pausanias.

name: Sanctuary of Hathor (Serabit el-Khadim)
description: Sanctuary of Hathor built in the Middle Kingdom at Serabit el-Khadim.

name: Sanctuary of Herakles Hippodetos
description: Pausanias describes the Sanctuary of Herakles Hippodetos located in the Teneric plain

name: Sanctuary of Isis at Dion
description: The sanctuary of Isis at Dion dates to the second century A.D.

name: Sanctuary of Kybele
description: A sanctuary dedicated to Kybele located at Daskalopetra on the island of Chios.

name: Sanctuary of Mercury Canetonnessis
description: Findspot of the so-called "Berthouville Treasure," discovered in 1830 in the hamlet of Villeret in the French commune of Berthouville. A temple and a theater of presumed Roman imperial date were subsequently excavated in the late 19th century. The site is not visible today, but a resistivity survey conducted in 2005 (Thierry) verified the locations of these major structures and the general accuracy of Babelon's map, deriving from the original excavations.

name: Sanctuary of Minerva at Spinera
description: A site sacred to Minerva with activity from the Early Iron Age onward, the Sanctuary of Minerva at Spinera is located at Breno on the banks of the river Oglio.

name: Sanctuary of Montélu
description: The Sanctuary of Montélu lies to the west of Chassenon. The building, only partially excavated, is of an octagonal plan and oriented to the cardinal points.

name: Sanctuary of Pan at Paneas
description: The Paneion at Caesarea Philippi, a sanctuary site dedicated to Pan centered around a natural cave and spring site on the southwestern slopess of Mount Hermon. The sanctuary was first built in the Hellenistic period (before 200 BCE). A temple to Augustus was constructed in front of the cave during the reign of Herod the Great in 19 BCE, and the site was further expanded through 220 CE.

name: Sanctuary of Poseidon at Kalaureia, Poros
description: A sanctuary sacred to Poseidon on the island of Poros.

name: Sanctuary of Poseidon at Tainaron
description: A famous ancient sanctuary of Poseidon located at the southern end of the modern Mani peninsula of the Peloponnese. Literary and archaeological evidence indicate that the sanctuary featured a temple, a cave treated as a mouth of the underworld, and other structural and monumental components. It seems to have served as a sanctuary site for suppliants and a context for the formal manumission of enslaved persons.

name: Sanctuary of Sol Indiges
description: The remains of the Sanctuary of Sol Indiges are located near Pomezia, Italy. The original phase of the sanctuary dates to the last quarter of the sixth century BCE, with subsequent rebuilding in the late fourth century BCE.

name: Sanctuary of the Muses
description: The Sanctuary of the Helikonian Muses in Boeotia is celebrated by Hesiod. Archaeological remains of part of the sanctuary in the valley of the Muses date to the third and second centuries B.C.

name: Sanctuary of Zeus Aphesios (Megarid)
description: Pausanias mentions the Sanctuary of Zeus Aphesios as being located atop a mountain. The site was excavated ca. 1890 and has views over the Saronic Gulf.

name: Sannabadae, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 66 E2 Sannabadae, Mon.

name: Sant'Ilario
description: A paleochristian site located at the thirtieth mile of the ancient via Labicana, near to the ancient road station 'ad Bivium'.

name: Santa Cristina
description: A nuragic sanctuary centered on a sacred well that was in use from ca. 1200 to 700 BC.

name: Santa Maria delle Vigne
description: A Paleochristian church (fifth century CE) located near ancient Lavinium.

name: Santa Maria di Devia
description: The church of Santa Maria di Devia is a Romanesque church of the eleventh century with Byzantine frescoes dating to the twelfth through fourteenth centuries.

name: Santa Maria in Legarano
description: Site of a large Roman villa, the remains of which were incorporated into the church of Santa Maria in Legarano. The early phases of the villa likely date to the early imperial period. At some point the villa became an Imperial property as it was donated to Pope Sylvester by Constantine.

name: Santa Maria in Portuno
description: A vicus and villa of the Roman period that lay along a route connecting the Via Flaminia to the coast. Archaeological evidence for the production of ceramics and tiles has been identified. A Christian monastery is established in 1090.

name: Santu Misirinu
description: An octagonal structure covered by a cupola, dating between the second and sixth centuries A.D.

name: Santuari Sanniti del Monte Cila
description: The ruins of a sanctuary associated with the Samnites located on Monte Cila, where a megalithic fortification is also attested.

name: Santuario dei Tre Santi (Alfio, Filadelfio e Cirino)
description: A church of ca. 1200 AD onwards, containing ancient material from the site of Apollonia in re-use.

name: Santuario di Fondo Patturelli
description: A Samnite sanctuary of ancient Capua that was initially discovered in 1845. Active from the sixth century B.C. until the first century B.C., the Santuario di Fondo Patturelli is noted for its architectural terracotta as well as for the presence of a sacred maternal cult.

name: Santuario di Maria Santissima del Lattani
description: A Marian sanctuary founded in 1430.

name: Santuario di Santa Maria di Barbana
description: The Santuario di Barbana is an ancient Marian shrine that dates ca. 582. It is said that Elia, the Patriarch of Aquileia, built a church near the hut of a hermit from Treviso by the name of Barbanus.

name: Santuario di Santa Maria Regina di Anglona
description: A Marian sanctuary located on the hill of Anglona in the province of Matera, Italy, that originates in the eleventh century.

name: Santuario nuragico di Santa Vittoria
description: A nuragic site near Serri, Sardinia, with a later phase of Christian re-use.

name: Sanxay
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 E2 Sanxay

name: São Miguel de Mota
description: Site of a temple dedicated to the Celtiberian/Lusitanian god Endovelicus.

name: Sar-i Pul
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 98 F2 Sar-i Pul

name: Satıköy
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 64 B4 Satıköy

name: Saturnus Balcaranensis, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 32 F3 Saturnus Balcaranensis, T.

name: Satyrou Mnema
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 87 L2 Satyrou Mnema

name: Scanno
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 44 E2 Scanno

name: Scargill Moors Roman shrines
description: A complex of two Roman shrines to the god Vintonus on Scargill Moor, near Bowes in County Durham.

name: Sceilg Mhichíl
description: A monastic complex on Skellig Michael established in the seventh century A.D. and enrolled as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1996.

name: Schiavi
description: Twin Italic temples located atop the Colle della Torre.

name: Scoglietto
description: A Roman sanctuary in use from the first century B.C. through fifth century A.D.

name: Scoppieto
description: An ancient settlement occupied from the fourth century B.C. to the fifth century A.D. noted for an Italic sanctuary and a production center for terra sigillata.

name: Seh Pa Aivan
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 92 B3 Seh Pa Aivan

name: Septeuil
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 11 B4 Septeuil

name: Serapeion
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 38 B2 Serapeion

name: Serapis, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 D3 Serapis, T.

name: Seuera, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 67 C4 Seuera, Mon.

name: Sghedeida
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 35 F2 Sghedeida

name: Shami
description: Site in the Shami Valley (Khuzestan Province, Iran), a settlement or sanctuary of the Parthian period (ca. 2nd century BCE-2nd century CE). In 1936, a team led by Marc Aurel Stein uncovered a bronze sculpture (the "Shami statue") at the site.

name: Shanhur
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 80 B2 Shanhur

name: Shara Temple at Umma
description: Large temple at Tell Jokha/Umma dedicated to the Sumerian god Shara. The temple was constructed by king Šu-Suen of the Third Dynasty of Ur (2035-2027 BCE), and was used during the Ur III-Old Babylonian periods.

name: Shepherds' Field
description: A chapel located in the Palestinian village of Beit Sahour that is believed to be the site of the annunciation of Jesus Christ's birth to the shepherds. A Christian monastery was founded on the site in the early fifth century.

name: Shrine of Dionysos
description: A rural shrine sacred to Dionysos located at Peristeria, Salamina in Salamis, Attica. The shrine is in the vicinity of the so-called "Cave of Euripides".

name: Shyrvallos
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 A3 Shyrvallos

name: Sidi Said
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 38 C1 no. 11 (Sidi Said)

name: Sidi-Mechrig
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 32 D2 Sidi-Mechrig

name: Sievering
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 13 B4 Sievering

name: Sigillyah
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 76 D4 Sigillyah

name: Sikinos (settlement)
description: The principal ancient settlement on the Aegean island of the same name. The ancient site is located where the church of Agia Marina now stands on a prominent height, about 5 km southwest of the modern town of Sikinos.

name: Silla de Papa church
description: The remains of a church belonging to the early Medieval period (perhaps the seventh century) that perhaps was a project of Pymenius, Bishop of Asidonia. This edifice is located on the hill that was the site of the oppidum referred to as Silla de Papa.

name: Sillingy
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 17 G2 Sillingy

name: Siniyah Island Monastery
description: A church and possible monastic complex identified in 2021/2022 on modern Siniyah Island in the emirate of Umm al-Quwain, UAE.

name: Sinuri, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 F3 Sinuri, T.

name: Sipahiler
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 86 C2 Sipahiler

name: Sirenae, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 44 F4 Sirenae, T.

name: Siret el-Craat
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 38 D1 no. 26 (Siret el-Craat)

name: Siret el-Giamel/Gasrin di Beida
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 38 C1 no. 9 (Siret el-Giamel/Gasrin di Beida)

name: Siret er-Rcheim
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 38 C1 no. 12 (Siret er-Rcheim)

name: Skala Polykhnitou
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 C3 Skala Polykhnitou

name: Skydra Hellenistic Temple
description: A Hellenistic temple, possibly of Zeus, the foundation of which was excavated on the southern outskirts of the residential area of modern Skydra in Greece.

name: Smintheion
description: Homer mentions the temple of Apollo Smintheus as the shrine where Chryses was priest. The second-century B.C. remains of an Ionic sanctuary with an adjacent town are located in the southwestern Troad near modern Gülpinar.

name: So-called Central Church in Apollonia
description: A three-aisled basilical church, with an external apse, situated near the centre of the ancient settlement of Apollonia (Cyrenaica), about half way between the East and West churches. The building has a narthex, a small western atrium and a range of added rooms along the north flank. The mosaics have been dated around the sixth century AD, but the building seems to have been built in earlier times (possibly fifth century AD).

name: So-called Temple 1 at Hattusa
description: Temple 1, also referred to as the "Great Temple", is located in the lower city of Hattusa. It dates to the thirteenth century B.C.

name: So-called twin sanctuary
description: A double sanctuary on the forum of Carsulae. It is no longer clear to which gods these temples belonged.

name: Sobek temple, Qaşr Qārūn
description: A Ptolemaic temple to the god Sobek.

name: Sofuentes
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 D3 Sofuentes

name: Soleb
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 81 A5 Soleb

name: Somapura Mahavihara
description: Somapura Mahavihara was an early medieval Buddhist monastery (vihara) established by king Dharmapala in the late eighth century or the early ninth century CE.

name: Son Bou Basilica
description: A three-aisled Paleochristian basilica of the fifth century A.D. located in the village of Son Bou in Menorca, Spain.

name: Songeons
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 11 B3 Songeons

name: Sorna Roman Villa and Church
description: Near Parentium (today Porec/Parenzo in Croatia), Cape Sorna (Zorna) first hosted a large villa maritima, and then a small early Christian church.

name: Souma el Kiata
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 34 F2 Souma el Kiata

name: Souma n'Ikhetteben
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 34 E2 Souma n'Ikhetteben

name: Soumat el Kheneg
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 33 B2 Soumat el Kheneg

name: Soyans
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 17 E4 Soyans

name: Sparta
description: Sparta was a prominent city-state (polis) of ancient Greece.

name: Spathari (temple)
description: A temple in Acarnania, located between Stratos (modern Sourovigli) and modern Lepenou. The temple seems to have been constructed in the Greek Archaic period on the site of an earlier Helladic settlement. The temple continued in operation into the Roman period.

name: Spelaion
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 54 C4 Spelaion

name: Speos Artemidos
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 D1 Speos Artemidos

name: Sri Ahobila Narasimha Swamy Temple
description: Main temple in Ahobilam dedicated to the man-lion form of the Hindu god Vishnu built or expanded in the 14th century, sacked by the Mughals in 1578, then restored and expanded at various times up to the present day.

name: Srir
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 67 D4 Srir

name: St Peters Church, Flawford
description: A ruined Medieval church located on the site of a Roman villa.

name: St-Alban-les-Eaux
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 17 B1 St-Alban-les-Eaux

name: St-Andéol
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 I4 St-Andéol

name: St-André-sur-Cailly
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 11 B3 St-André-sur-Cailly

name: St-Aubin-sur-Gaillon
description: St-Aubin-sur-Gaillon was the site of a Gallo-Roman sanctuary.

name: St-Aubin-sur-Mer
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 7 F2 St-Aubin-sur-Mer

name: St-Béat
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 F3 St-Béat (1)

name: St-Estève-de-Ménerbes
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 15 E2 St-Estève-de-Ménerbes

name: St-Galmier
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 17 C2 St-Galmier

name: St-Gervais
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 D2 St-Gervais

name: St-Goussard
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 G2 St-Goussard

name: St-Laurent-les-Bains
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 17 B4 St-Laurent-les-Bains

name: St-Maurice-d’Ibie
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 17 C5 St-Maurice-d’Ibie

name: St-Victor
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 H2 St-Victor

name: St. Ulrich
description: An archaeological site first excavated between 1894 and 1897 that has yielded remains of several villas, including a luxury villa of more than 117 rooms, and a sanctuary.

name: Stancija Blek
description: The archaeological site of Stancija Blek (Tar-Vabrega, Istria-Croatia) is located on the west coast of Istria. In antiquity, the site would have been located in the territory of Parentium. Archaeological investigation has documented more than 1,500 years of occupation, beginning perhaps with a Roman-period agricultural establishment (villa) that was subsequently followed by Late Antique and Medieval phases that include a so-called tower. Nearby are the remains of the church of Santa Croce.

name: Ste-Abeille
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 17 C4 Ste-Abeille

name: Ste-Barbe-du-Tlélat
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 29 E1 Ste-Barbe-du-Tlélat

name: Ste-Cécile Paleochristian Church
description: Remains of this paleochristian church, located at modern Loupian in Hérault, France, were discovered and excavated in the 1980s. The structure lies immediately north of the current church of Ste-Cécile, which dates to the 14th century.

name: Ste-Ruffine
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 11 G3 Ste-Ruffine

name: Stixneusiedl
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 13 B4 Stixneusiedl

name: Su Tempiesu
description: A nuragic spring sanctuary in use from the thirteenth to ninth centuries BC.

name: Sudelfels
description: The site of a Gallo-Roman sanctuary.

name: Suk el-Oti
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 35 G3 Suk el-Oti

name: Sun Temple of Niuserre
description: Temple dedicated to the god Ra in the 5th dynasty by the pharaoh Niuserre (Nyuserre).

name: Surb Tadeos
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 89 G1 Surb Tadeos

name: Synagogue at Aḥmadiah (Aḥmadiya, Ahmadiyye)
description: Remains of a synagogue in the Golan with Greek and Hebrew Inscriptions

name: Syreanos
description: An ancient sanctuary of Zeus attested in inscriptions found on "the upland plain of Makas Alan between the [Phrygian] cities of Kotiaeion (Kiitahya) and Nakoleia (Seyitgazi)" in modern Turkey (Drew-Bear). Its precise location cannot be determined today.

name: Ta Prohm
description: Khmer temple at Angkor constructed in 1186 CE under Jayavarman VII.

name: Ta' Ħaġrat Temple Complex
description: Ta' Ħaġrat is a megalithic temple complex on the island of Malta (ca. 3600-3000 B.C.), included in the UNESCO World Heritage List.

name: Tabennisis
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 80 A1 Tabennisis

name: Taberna Frigida
description: Taberna Frigida was a road station on the Via Aemilia Scauri at the crossing of the Frigidus river, located between Pisae and Luna.

name: Tabernae
description: Remains of a Roman vicus and hilltop temple complex excavated at modern Tawern in Germany.

name: Tabo
description: A Kushite site located on Argo Island in Nubia, the location of a large Amun temple.

name: Tagai
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 66 D4 Tagai

name: Takht-i Bilqis
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 90 C4 Takht-i Bilqis

name: Takht-i Rustam
description: At Takht-i Rustam is a bedrock-carved stupa-monastery complex.

name: Tal-i Hakavan
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 94 C4 Tal-i Hakavan

name: Tall Bāzy
description: Archaeological site on the eastern bank of the Euphrates in northern Syria. The site contains a town dated to the Early Bronze Age III-IVA (mid-3rd millennium BCE), as well as a Roman sanctuary used only briefly from about the late 1st-late 2nd century CE. The western part of the site was flooded by the construction of the Tischrin Dam in 1999. Tall Bazi and the nearby site of Banat have been tentatively identified as the city of Armi/Armium mentioned in texts from Ebla.

name: Tallaion Antron
description: The Melidoni Cave - Gerontospilios.

name: Tang-i Qandil
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 94 B4 Tang-i Qandil

name: Tang-i Sarvak
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 93 G3 Tang-i Sarvak

name: Taq-i Gunbad
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 94 C5 Taq-i Gunbad

name: Tara
description: Tara is an archaeological site comprising a number of ancient monuments. Tradition holds that the site was the seat of the High King of Ireland.

name: Tarasov Mound Site
description: Area with 17 Early Iron Age mounds located near the modern village Tarasovo and the Kama river in Russia's Udmurtia state. All mounds had settlements except Tarasov I, which was the location of a religious sanctuary and cemetery. The site has been partially destroyed by oil digging in the area.

name: Tas-Silġ
description: A hilltop on Malta that is the locus of multi-period settlement and religious activity from the Neolithic period to late antiquity.

name: Tatev
description: A ninth-century monastery located on a large basalt plateau in southeastern Armenia.

name: Taxiarkhis
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 B3 Taxiarkhis

name: Tchonemyris
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 79 D3 Tchonemyris

name: Tebedut
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 35 E2 Tebedut

name: Tekmoreioi
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 62 F5 Tekmoreioi

name: Telfes
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 19 D2 Telfes

name: Tell F6 at Failaka
description: Tell on the southwestern corner of Failaka Island containing the remains of two large buildings, tentatively identified as a palace and a temple, dated to 1900-1400 BCE.

name: Tell Umm Amer
description: The archaeological site of Tell Umm Amer in the Gaza Strip is included on the Madaba Map under the name "Tabatha". The site was the birthplace in 291 CE of Saint Hilarion who founded an eponymous monastery there. The monastery was destroyed in 614.

name: Temenos of Athena
description: The area on the northeast side of the Piana di Himera is delimited by walls and contains four religious buildings (temples A, B, C, and D), probably dedicated to Athena. The foundations are still visible in the archaeological site of Himera.

name: Temes
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 24 D2 Temes

name: Tempelbezirk Pesch
description: A Gallo-Roman temple complex dated to the 1st-4th centuries CE.

name: Tempio di Grasceta dei Cavallari
description: The temple of Grasceta dei Cavallari is an Etrusco-Roman sanctuary located in the municipality of Tolfa. This sanctuary sits at the foot of Mount Mazza, above a pass near Tarquinia and Caere's ancient borders. The temple was small, with a rectangular platform and an octastyle arrangement.

name: Temple at Santa Maria in Canale
description: A temple of Tuscan type with cella and lateral alae built of ashlar masonry and dated to the 3rd century BCE, now built into a rural church and farmstead complex. The temple is situated along the Via Amerina between Ameria and Tuder, and marks the boundary between those two Umbrian communities. A fragmentary Umbrian bronze inscription (Vetter 229) of around 300 BCE mentioning a deity characterized as "Iovia" was discovered here in the 18th century CE.

name: Temple C at Selinus
description: The so-called Temple C at Selinus is a Doric temple that dates to the second half of the sixth century B.C.

name: Temple complex (Petinesca)
description: A Gallo-Roman sanctuary with an enclosed precinct that was linked to the oppidum.

name: Temple complex at Kommos
description: Multi-phase temple site in the city of Kommos in southern Crete. Temple A was constructed by the late 11th century CE. A larger structure, Temple B, was built over it in the 9th century CE. The temple complex reached its maximum extent when Temple C was built over the site and its surroundings in the 7th century CE.

name: Temple d'Appolon
description: The ruins of a Roman-period temple dedicated to Apollo located at the Oppidum de Gaujac (Gard, France).

name: Temple of "Artemis Epikrateia"
description: A classical/hellenistic temple located in the likely territory of Acarnanian Alyzeia. The temple site lies over 6km southeast from the ancient walled city (about a kilometer south of modern Paliovarka, Greece) at a site known in modern times as Drymonas Archontochoriou. It is perched on rising ground with a view westward to the channel and the island of Karnos (modern Kalamos). Inscriptions have been recovered from the site, and an unlooted portion of a votive deposit was scientifically excavated in the 1990s.

name: Temple of Amada
description: The Temple of Amada is the oldest Egyptian temple known in Nubia. Founded by Pharaoh Thutmose III (18th dynasty), the temple was dedicated to Amun and Re-Horakhty. The decorative program is attributed to Amenhotep II, his son and successor. The temple was relocated to its current position in 1964 along with the Temple of Derr.

name: Temple of Amun at Jebel Barkal
description: Large temple dedicated to the Amun located at the base of Jebel Barkal. Designated temple B500, the structure was likely constructed by the Napatan king Piankhi in the 8th century BCE.

name: Temple of Aphrodite Euploia
description: The temple of Aphrodite Euploia is a tholos constructed in the fourth century BC in Knidos, a coastal city on the Datça Peninsula. It was built to house Praxiteles' famous nude statue of Aphrodite.

name: Temple of Apollo at Metropolis
description: A peripteral Doric temple built in the mid-6th c. BCE and destroyed by fire in the 2nd c. BCE. The temple faces east, and was built of mudbrick on foundations of local sandstone, with sandstone column drums and a terracotta roof. A 4th c. BCE dedicatory inscription found in the cella attests the worship of Apollo. The structure was discovered in 1994 following the activity of looters.

name: Temple of Apollo at Tempe
description: The remains of an ancient temple with inscriptions naming Apollo Pythios were revealed by infrastructure works in 1957. These remains were located in the Tempe gorge, opposite the church of Agia Paraskevi.

name: Temple of Apollo at Thermos
description: A temple to the Apollo was built in the late seventh century B.C. atop the ruins of the previous megaron in Thermos.

name: Temple of Apollo Lepsios
description: A temple of Apollo on the Aegean island of Lepsia, generally thought to have been located in the vicinity of the modern church of Hagios Nikolaos. It is attested in various ancient sources, including a recently published epigraphic document demarcating the temple lands.

name: Temple of Apollo Pythios
description: The Hellenistic Temple of Apollo Pythios on the Acropolis of the city of Rhodes.

name: Temple of Apollo Zoster
description: The Temple of Apollo Zoster is a sanctuary of the god Apollo located in Vouliagmeni in Attica, Greece. The temple's foundations date to the sixth century BC.

name: Temple of Artemis (Aulis)
description: Archaic temple to Artemis at Aulis, Boeotia. In mythology, said to be the place of death of Iphigenia. Mentioned in Euripides' 'Iphigenia at Aulis.'

name: Temple of Artemis (Thasos)
description: Temple of Artemis on Thasos Island with remains from the Archaic period to the 1st century CE.

name: Temple of Artemis Orthosia
description: The Temple of Artemis Orthosia at Epidauros was founded in the Archaic period and continued in use in the Roman period. Pausanias mentions it as being on the city's temples.

name: Temple of Asclepios
description: The Asklepieion at Lissus, Crete.

name: Temple of Athena (Ilium)
description: The Temple of Athena at Ilion was a peripteral temple executed in the Doric order with a 12 x 6 columnar arrangement. Its creation is traditionally ascribed to Lysimachus (ca. 301-281 BCE), though it was later restored under Roman influence, likely during the principate of Augustus. Little of the temple, save its forecourt, remains in situ. It is mostly known from membra disiecta, including metopes uncovered during Schliemann's excavations.

name: Temple of Athena at Assos
description: A Late Archaic peripteral temple in the Doric order with a 6 x 13 columnar arrangement.

name: Temple of Athena Makistos
description: An Athena temple located to the west of Nea Skilloundia, possibly at ancient Makiston, in the Peloponnese.

name: Temple of Athena Pallenis
description: The Temple of Athena Pallenis was built in the mid-6th century BC and rebuilt on a larger scale in the late 5th century BC. In myth, the location was the site of King Eurystheus' tomb and Theseus' victory over the Pallantidai. In 546 BC, it was the site of the battle by which Peisistratos finally established his tyranny. At the end of the 1st century BC, the structure was disassembled and rebuilt in the Agora of Athens as the Temple of Ares.

name: Temple of Athena Polias
description: Pausanias describes the Temple of Athena Polias at Erythrai as containing a wooden image of the enthroned goddess.

name: Temple of Athena Skiras at Phaleron
description: This sanctuary, reported in Phaleron by Pausanias, is thought to have been located near the modern "old town" of Phaleron in Attica. It is not to be confused with the Temple of Athena Skiras on the island of Salamis, reported by Herodotus.

name: Temple of Athena Skiras on Salamis
description: This sanctuary, reported by Herodotus, is thought to have been located on the modern Cape Arapis in the northeast part of the island of Salamis, just north of the modern naval base. It is not to be confused with the Temple of Athena Skiras in Phaleron, reported by Pausanias.

name: Temple of Augustales at Centuripae
description: An early Imperial (first to third centuries) temple at Centuripae that has been associated with the priestly college of the Augustales.

name: Temple of Cybele
description: The Attalids built a temple at Pessinus to honor Cybele.

name: Temple of Dakka
description: An ancient temple relocated in the 1960s before the completion of the Aswan dam.

name: Temple of Demeter
description: A Doric temple ascribed to Demeter with origins in the fourth century B.C.

name: Temple of Demeter and Kore
description: A Punic and Roman temple sacred to Demeter and Kore located at Bagoi, Perdaxius, Sardinia.

name: Temple of Dionysos at Teos
description: A Temple of Dionysos in the Ionic order begun in the Hellenistic period.

name: Temple of Ellesyia
description: A small rock-cut temple dedicated to Amun, Horus, and Satis dated to the 18th Dynasty. Originally located near Qasr Ibrim/Premis, the temple was relocated to the Museo Egizio in Turin, Italy, in the 1960s prior to the area's flooding beneath the surface of Lake Nasser.

name: Temple of Enodia
description: Pherai was the main cult center of the goddess Enodia. Today, the foundations of the temple of Enodia (or Ennodia) at found at the site.

name: Temple of Fortuna (Jublains)
description: Centerpoint of sanctuary at modern Jublains

name: Temple of Hera at Foce del Sele
description: A sanctuary at the mouth of the Sele river in Italy with an octastyle temple dedicated to the Argive Hera.

name: Temple of Hera Lacinia
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 46 F3 Hera, T.

name: Temple of Hercules
description: Remains of the fourth-century BCE Temple of Hercules are preserved in the edifice of San Marco d'Alunzio.

name: Temple of Hercules at Amman/Philadelpheia
description: The Temple of Hercules is located within the Amman Citadel in Amman, Jordan. The temple was built during the administration of Geminius Marcianus, ca. 162-166.

name: Temple of Hercules at Montorio al Vomano
description: A sanctuary of Hercules located in the territory of Teramo near the Vomano river.

name: Temple of Hercules, Lanuvium
description: The Temple of Hercules at Lanuvium.

name: Temple of Iuno Moneta at Signia
description: The remains of the Temple of Iuno Moneta on the acropolis of Segni support the later (tenth century) church of St. Peter.

name: Temple of Jupiter (Silifke)
description: A second-century CE temple sacred to Jupiter.

name: Temple of Jupiter?, Via Appia
description: A temple located at the fourth mile of the Via Appia.

name: Temple of Mater Matuta at Satricum
description: A sanctuary dedicated to Mater Matuta that was in use between the seventh and fifth centuries B.C.

name: Temple of Min and Isis
description: This temple is located at the modern Egyptian village of El-Qala, which is near Coptos (modern Qift). It was built likely under Augustus, expanded by Caligula and Claudius, and survived into the Coptic period.

name: Temple of Minerva at Theveste
description: The Temple of Minerva at Theveste dates to the third century A.D.

name: Temple of Montu
description: Egyptian temple dedicated to the god Montou in Armant, built and enlarged from the Middle Kingdom to the Roman period.

name: Temple of Montu
description: Egyptian temple dedicated to the god Montou in Medamud, built and enlarged from the Middle Kingdom to the Roman period.

name: Temple of Montu (El-Tod)
description: A temple sacred to Montu located at El-Tod, Egypt. The temple's foundations were laid during the Fifth Dynasty and the sanctuary continued in use under the Ptolemies.

name: Temple of Poseidon and Amphitrite on Tenos
description: A peripteral temple.

name: Temple of Sardus Pater
description: A Punic-Roman temple dedicated to Sardus Pater Babai located near Fluminimaggiore in southern Sardinia. The original phase of the temple dates ca. 500 BC.

name: Temple of Soknebtunis
description: A temple dedicated to a local form of the Egyptian god Sobek, Soknebtunis, at Tebtunis. The temple was built by Ptolemy I in the late fourth-early third centuries and was greatly expanded by Ptolemy XII in the first century BCE.

name: Temple of Talamonaccio
description: An Etruscan temple of the fourth century BCE, associated with the ancient settlement of Telamon. It lies on Mount Talamonaccio, across the bay from the modern village of Talamone, north of Orbetello, Italy.

name: Temple of the Oxus
description: The Temple of the Oxus is a Seleucid-era temple situated within the citadel of Takhti-Sangin, a Graeco-Bactrian town between the Vakhsh and Panj rivers.

name: Temple of the Storm God at Aleppo
description: Bronze Age temple dedicated to the weather god Hadad in the citadel of Halab (modern Aleppo).

name: Temple of Thelsae
description: A well-preserved Roman period temple located in modern Dmeir, Syria. The sanctuary was later converted into a fortification.

name: Temple of Trajan at Italica
description: A sanctuary dedicated to Trajan in the 'urbs nova' of Italica that flourished under the patronage of Hadrian.

name: Temple of Zeus
description: The Temple of Zeus is Aizanoi's main sanctuary. The temple complex was built under Domitian.

name: Temple of Zeus at Lebadea
description: The foundations of a temple sacred to Zeus at Lebadea. Pausanias reports that the temple was incomplete, owing perhaps to its over-ambitious size or to the nagging costs associated with warfare.

name: Temple of Zeus Karaos
description: The foundations of an ancient temple located to the west of ancient Astakos/Dragomestre in Aitoloakarnania.

name: Temple of Zeus Lepsinos at Euromus
description: The Temple of Zeus Lepsinos at Euromus is located southeast of the ancient settlement. The well-preserved Corinthian temple dates to the reign of Hadrian.

name: Temple of Zeus Megistos, Canatha
description: A tetrastyle temple in the Corinthian order sacred to Zeus Megistos was built at Canatha by the first century CE.

name: Temple of Zeus Trossou
description: An epigraphically attested temple at or near ancient Atyochorion (modern Akkent in Turkey).

name: Terrace and altar of M. Nonius Balbus
description: Marcus Nonius Balbus of Nuceria was an important civic patron of Herculaneum. Balbus was a praetor and proconsul of Crete and Cyrene and served as tribune of the plebs in 32 B.C. This architectural complex near the Suburban Baths included the terrace as well as an honorific statue and altar.

name: Terrace of Zeus
description: The so-called "Terrace of Zeus" or "Terrazza di Zeus" at Elea-Velia.

name: Thalfang-Bäsch
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 11 G3 Thalfang-Bäsch

name: Thaur
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 19 D2 Thaur

name: The "Hochtor Sanctuary"
description: A Celto-Roman sanctuary situated at an ancient high-mountain pass in the eastern Alps near Grossglockner, excavated beginning in the 1990s. Its ancient name is unknown.

name: The Sanctuary of the Nymph Ionides
description: Healing spring described by Pausanias on the Kytherios River, in Salmone, Elis

name: The Sanctuary of Vastogirardi
description: A sanctuary of the Pentrian Samnites on the flanks of Monte Capraro, north of the modern Italian village of Vastogirardi.

name: Thea Larmene, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 H4 Thea Larmene, T.

name: Theai kai Nymphai, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 60 D5 Theai kai Nymphai, T.

name: Theater-temple complex, Monte San Nicola
description: A theater-temple complex that stands within pre-Roman walls belonging to a period of Samnite occupation on Monte San Nicola in the municipality of Pietravairano.

name: Theodosios
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 70 G2 Theodosios

name: Theoktistos
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 70 G2 Theoktistos

name: Theotokos Kosmosoteira
description: A twelfth-century monastic foundation and fortified church in the modern town of Feres in Greece's Evros prefecture. The structure served as a mosque during the Ottoman empire and was returned to use as a church in the 20th century.

name: Thermai
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 E4 Thermai

name: Thetideion
description: A sanctuary of Thetis and/or settlement in the ancient territory of Pharsalus. Scholars have suggested various locations for the ancient site, including Ag. Athanassion at Kato Dasolophos (formerly Bekides) and the area of modern Thetidion (formerly Alchami).

name: Thisbe
description: Thisbe was an ancient town of Boeotia located on the southern flank of Mt. Helicon.

name: Thistleton Roman Town
description: A small town of the Roman era, with likely Iron Age antecedent and associated farmsteads and field systems, located near and around modern Thistleton, Market Overton, and Kendrew Barracks (the former RAF Cottesmore) in Rutland district, England. This archaeological landscape has been known and explored since at least the 19th century through chance finds, field walking, geophysical prospecting, targeted excavation, and aerial photography.

name: Tholas
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 76 D4 Tholas

name: Thomas, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 67 B4 Thomas, Mon.

name: Tigani basilica
description: An Early Christian basilica dating to the fifth and sixth centuries CE.

name: Tigi
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 35 D2 Tigi

name: Timpone della Motta
description: The Timpone della Motta is an archaeological site located near Francavilla Marittima. The site was inhabited from the Middle Bronze Age onward. During the Iron Age, an Oenotrian settlement flourished here, one that developed into an important sanctuary. The site passed into Greek control in the later seventh century B.C. and was eventually abandoned following the Bruttian conquest of the region during the fourth century B.C.

name: Tininai
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 35 F3 Tininai

name: Titkois
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 D1 Titkois

name: Tongeren Gallo-Roman temple complex
description: The Gallo-Roman temple complex of Tongeren is located in the northwestern part of the city of Tongeren. The site pre-dates the Roman city wall.

name: Tor Tignosa
description: Tor Tignosa, mapped and listed in the 17th century Catasto Alessandrino as a landed estate, is a ruined stone structure located some 8 km northeast of Lavinium. Multiple votive inscriptions were found in the vicinity, providing evidence for an ancient sanctuary dating to between the fifth and second centuries BCE.

name: Torre del Fum
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 I4 Torre del Fum

name: Torricella
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 45 G4 Torricella

name: Tossal del Moro
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 F4 Tossal del Moro

name: Tou Batou
description: A monastery built by order of Emperor Justinian I. St. Catherine's at the foot of Mount Sinai in modern Egypt.

name: Toumpa
description: Iron Age sanctuary site located approximately 4.5 km south of the city of Salamis.

name: Tour de Gresset
description: The remains of a Gallo-Roman fanum.

name: Trakhonas
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 D2 Trakhonas

name: Trapeza/Koumari temple (Achaia)
description: A temple in the Doric order that begins in the sixth century BCE and continues in use until the Hellenistic period. The temple may be associated with ancient Rhypai, at Trapeza near Koumari, Aigio, Achaia.

name: Trigh-Tarhuna
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 35 F2 Trigh-Tarhuna

name: Tripolis
description: The Phoenicians established a commercial outpost at Tripolis (Tripoli) in the ninth century BC. An independent city during the Hellenistic period, Tripolis came under Roman control in 64 BC.

name: Troulli tis Korfis
description: A hilltop sanctuary, in use from the Bronze Age until the time of the Roman Empire.

name: Tura d’ Urhay
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 67 H2 Tura d’ Urhay

name: Tutzis
description: Toutzis (Dendur) is located in Nubia, approximately 80 km south of modern Aswan. In 23 BCE the Roman provincial governor Petronius built a temple at the direction of Augustus. The temple was dedicated to Isis and the deified Nubian brothers Pedesi and Pihor. Imperiled by the Aswan High Dam project, the temple was relocated by UNESCO in 1963. Egypt gifted the temple to the Metropolitan Museum of Art in New York City, where it has been exhibited since 1978.

name: Tzitzis
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 81 C2 Tzitzis

name: Unnamed Gallo-Roman temple
description: The site of an unnamed Gallo-Roman temple is situated in Thésée, Arrondissement of Romorantin-Lanthenay, Centre-Val de Loire, France. This temple site is located close to the ruins at Bâtiments des potiers de Pouillé.

name: Unnamed monastery
description: A monastery founded in late antiquity on the island then known as Lerina/Planasia (modern Île Saint-Honorat, France; BAtlas 16 D2 unnamed monastery)

name: Unnamed place
description: An unnamed monument, settlement, or villa of Syrtica

name: Unnamed Place
description: An unnamed place of Tripolitana

name: Unnamed religious complex
description: Referred to today as "Gisacum", this vast Gallo-Roman religious complex (with major spa), is located largely in the French commune of Le Vieil Evreux.

name: Unnamed sanctuary at Garenne
description: In modern Moulin des Gaumins in commune Thaumiers, France (BAtlas 14 H2 Thaumiers)

name: Unnamed temple
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 E2 no. 62 (temple NW Anagnia)

name: Unnamed temple (Tre Fontane)
description: M. Cristofani describes terracotta antefixes discovered at Tre Fontane, in the general location of the abbey of the same name. These antefixes form part of a set of evidence relevant to a discussion of the functioning of regional coroplastic workshops in central Italy in the late archaic period. They are dated from the later sixth to the early fifth centuries BCE.

name: Untitled
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 38 D1 no. 21 (Ras el-Hilal)

name: Ursins Gallo-Roman temple
description: Site of a Gallo-Roman temple.

name: Vacri Italic temple
description: An Italic temple was identified in 1975 in the locality known as Porcareccia, Vacri (Chieti), Italy.

name: Vagniacis
description: Vagniacis was a small Roman site of the first to fifth centuries with likely specialized religious functions. A series of five temples are situated within a temenos along the Watling Street at Springhead.

name: Val-des-Nymphes
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 17 D5 Val-des-Nymphes

name: Varces-Allières-et-Risset
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 17 F3 Varces-Allières-et-Risset

name: Vardzia
description: Cave monastery complex in southern Georgia constructed from 1184-1186.

name: Varnenum
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 11 G2 Varnenum

name: Vaugrenier
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 16 D2 Vaugrenier

name: Vega del Mar
description: A paleochristian basilica.

name: Velletri-Stimmate
description: At Velletri the remains of a Late Archaic sanctuary (ca. 530 BCE) were discovered beneath the church of the SS. Stimmate di S. Francesco. The site is often referred to as "Tempio delle Stimmate"; Cardinal Stefano Borgia discovered it in 1784. The architectural terracottas from the sanctuary are part of the so-called "Veio-Roma-Velletri" decorative system.

name: Velousia
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 G4 Velousia

name: Verbinum/Vironum
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 11 D3 Verbinum/Vironum

name: Vercoiran
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 17 E5 Vercoiran

name: Vessagiriya
description: Vessagiriya is the site of an ancient Buddhist forest monastery in Sri Lanka. It is considered part of the ruined site of Anuradhapura. The monastery was initially built in the middle of the third century BCE and was expanded in the late fifth century CE. A Roman bronze coin, as well as punch-marked coins and/or ingots, were excavated at Vessagiriya in the first decade of the 20th century.

name: Vietheil
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 G3 Vietheil

name: Villa gallo-romaine de Barat de Vin
description: Rediscovered in the 19th century and partially excavated in the 1960s, this Roman-era complex east of modern Sorde-l'Abbaye (Landes, Nouvelle-Aquitaine in France) has been variously identified as a villa and a road station. Discoveries at the site included stone walls, a courtyard, a portico, baths, a mosaic floor, and a bordering Roman-era roadway. A medieval church indicated in documents and early maps seems to have used some of the ancient structures. The site appears to be largely overgrown and inaccessible today.

name: Villa S. Silvestro
description: The remains of a Republican temple podium now support a Christian church at Villa S. Silvestro in Umbria. The temple's podium likely dates, in its first phase, to the third century B.C.

name: Villards-d’Heria
description: A sanctuary of the Sequani at modern Villards-d'Heria in France. It is archaeologically represented by a two-part architectural complex comprising (1) north of the modern town the monumental "Pont des Arches", which supported a temple over a watercourse that supplied a large spa, and (2) at the nearby Lac-d'Antre, which supplied the majority of the water for the spa complex and also hosted additional temples along its shoreline. Plans in Vurpillot 2016 and Nouvel 2019.

name: Visigothic basilica at Segobriga
description: The basilica of Cabeza del Griego at Segobriga dates to the fifth and sixth centuries CE.

name: Vittel

description: The Yeha Temple is alleged to be among the oldest known extant structures in Ethiopia. It is executed in the Sabaean style and may date to the seventh century BCE.

name: Yeşilalıç rock niche inscription
description: The modern village formerly known as Ašotakert and/or Aschrut-Darga is situated ca. 60 km to the east of Van at an altitude of 2600 m above the stream Çaybağı which flows into the river Kotur in Iran. Here a rock niche with an inscription of the Urartian kings Išpuini (9th century BCE) and Minua (9th / 8th century BCE) was found. It reports the construction of a "susi temple" of the god Ḫaldi and the establishment of an offering ritual. In this case, the term "susi temple" apparently refers to the niche.
